alignments.preprint_claim_text: NS-Forest v4.0 outperforms previous versions on its ability to identify markers with higher On-Target Fraction values for closely related cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NS-Forest v4.0 outperforms previous versions in simulation studies and on its ability to identify markers with higher On-Target Fraction values for closely related cell types in real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim but adds that this is supported by simulation studies and real-data analyses, clarifying and broadening the evidence context while keeping the confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NS-Forest v4.0 outperforms other marker gene selection approaches at classification with significantly higher F-beta scores when applied to datasets from three human organs - brain, kidney, and lung. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NS-Forest v4.0 outperforms other marker gene selection approaches for cell type classification with significantly higher F-beta scores when applied to datasets from three human organs—brain, kidney, and lung. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative classification result with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The On-Target Fraction metric quantifies how well the identified markers exhibit the desired pattern of being exclusively expressed at high levels within their target cell types. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The On-Target Fraction metric introduced quantifies how well the identified markers exhibit the desired pattern of being exclusively expressed at high levels within their target cell types. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same definition/description of the metric; wording and meaning are unchanged though its presentation is as a published result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CENsible can effectively distinguish active vs. inactive compounds for many systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CENsible can effectively distinguish active vs inactive compounds for many systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CENsible retains interpretability, allowing researchers to identify the contribution of each pre-calculated term to the final affinity prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CENsible retains interpretability, allowing researchers to identify the contribution of each precalculated term to the final affinity prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The interpretability claim is carried forward unchanged in meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The primary benefit of CENsible over related machine-learning scoring functions is its interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The primary benefit of CENsible over related machine-learning scoring functions is its interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same comparative benefit claim without substantive alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DrugHIVE is a deep hierarchical structure-based generative model that enables fine-grained control over molecular generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DrugHIVE's hierarchical design enables improved control over molecular generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution of hierarchical design enabling enhanced control; wording differs slightly but the claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DrugHIVE outperforms state of the art autoregressive and diffusion-based methods on common benchmarks and in speed of generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DrugHIVE outperforms state-of-the-art autoregressive and diffusion-based methods in both speed and performance on common generative benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same comparative performance and speed advantages on benchmarks; content and confidence are consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DrugHIVE can accelerate a wide range of common drug design tasks such as de novo generation, molecular optimization, scaffold hopping, linker design, and high throughput pattern replacement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DrugHIVE dramatically increases virtual screening efficiency and accelerates a wide range of common drug design tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim by emphasizing dramatic improvements in virtual screening efficiency in addition to accelerating many design tasks, broadening impact and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is highly scalable and can be applied to high confidence AlphaFold predicted receptors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DrugHIVE can be applied to receptors with high-confidence AlphaFold-predicted structures, extending the ability to generate high-quality drug-like molecules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms applicability to AlphaFold-predicted receptors and explicitly ties this to generating high-quality molecules; the core applicability claim is preserved and slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DrugHIVE extends the ability to generate high quality drug-like molecules to a majority of the unsolved human proteome. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DrugHIVE can be applied to receptors with high-confidence AlphaFold-predicted structures, extending the ability to generate high-quality drug-like molecules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the extension to generate high-quality molecules but omits the stronger claim about covering a 'majority of the unsolved human proteome,' effectively narrowing and revising that earlier, broader assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ThermoMPNN is a deep neural network trained to predict stability changes for protein point mutations given an initial structure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ThermoMPNN is a deep neural network trained to predict stability changes for protein point mutations given an initial structure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same model description with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The utility of a newly released mega-scale stability dataset for training a robust stability model is demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The utility of a recently released megascale stability dataset for training a robust stability model is demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim the dataset's utility for training; wording differences do not change meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method achieves competitive performance on established benchmark datasets using a lightweight model architecture. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on established benchmark datasets using a lightweight model architecture. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the performance claim from 'competitive' to 'state-of-the-art' and increases certainty, constituting a substantive revision and amplification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ThermoMPNN is made readily available as a tool for stability prediction and design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ThermoMPNN is made readily available as a tool for stability prediction and design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The availability claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Riboformer is a deep learning-based framework that models context-dependent changes in translation dynamics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Riboformer is a deep learning-based framework that models context-dependent changes in translation dynamics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Riboformer accurately predicts ribosome densities at codon resolution and corrects experimental artifacts in previously unseen datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Riboformer accurately predicts ribosome densities at codon resolution and corrects experimental artifacts in previously unseen datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictive accuracy and artifact-correction claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Riboformer reveals subtle differences in synonymous codon translation and uncovers a bottleneck in protein synthesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Riboformer reveals subtle differences in synonymous codon translation and uncovers a bottleneck in translation elongation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines 'protein synthesis' to the more specific 'translation elongation', clarifying the mechanistic locus without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Riboformer can be combined with in silico mutagenesis analysis to identify sequence motifs that contribute to ribosome stalling across various biological contexts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Riboformer can identify sequence motifs that contribute to ribosome stalling across various biological contexts, including aging and viral infection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the motif-identification claim but specifies example contexts (aging, viral infection), clarifying and broadening the contexts where the claim applies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RiPP cyclodehydratase, LynD, exhibits broad substrate promiscuity that can be measured and predicted using high throughput assays.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prototypical YcaO cyclodehydratase, LynD, exhibits substrate promiscuity that can be examined using mRNA display technology.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert LynD substrate promiscuity and experimental interrogation, but the publication specifies mRNA display as the assay, narrowing the original general 'high throughput assays' phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: mRNA display technology allows for extensive substrate profiling of LynD, which aids in the construction of deep learning models for predicting substrate processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning model has been constructed to accurately predict substrate processing by LynD, explaining specific combinations of epistatic interactions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim that mRNA display enables model building is realized in the publication's explicit statement that a predictive deep learning model was constructed; the publication focuses more on the resulting model performance and explanations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning models developed from mRNA display data provide insights into epistatic interactions involved in enzymatic processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning model has been constructed to accurately predict substrate processing by LynD, explaining specific combinations of epistatic interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the model explains specific epistatic interactions, strengthening and clarifying the preprint's more tentative insight into epistasis (preprint rated medium certainty, publication high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research will facilitate the elucidation of other RiPP enzymes and their application in modifying mRNA display libraries for therapeutic development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The understanding of substrate scope and tolerance of cyclodehydratases can facilitate their use in modifying mRNA display libraries for new inhibitor and therapeutic selection.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert facilitation of using cyclodehydratases for modifying mRNA display libraries toward therapeutics; the publication is slightly more specific about inhibitor/therapeutic selection.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Charged residues are disfavored in proximity to the residue undergoing modification by LynD, as are multiple adjacent heterocyclizations.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific substrate preference finding (charged residues and adjacent heterocyclizations) that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The csPWS-seg approach achieved a median Intersection over Union (IoU) of 0.80 and a Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) score of 0.88 for nuclei segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The csPWS-seg approach achieved a median intersection over union (IoU) of 0.80 and a Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) score of 0.89 for nuclei segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance is reported in both, but the DSC changed slightly from 0.88 to 0.89 in the publication, so the claim is revised with an updated metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The csPWS-seg model demonstrated superior performance over the baseline U-Net model and another attention-based model, SE-U-Net.
alignments.pub_claim_text: csPWS-seg outperformed several commonly used deep learning-based segmentation models for biomedical imaging, marking a significant improvement in segmentation accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's comparison (named baselines) to a broader set of commonly used models, broadening and clarifying the scope of the outperforming claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The csPWS-seg with focal loss provided the best results compared to other loss functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a combination of focal loss and other loss functions resulted in the best performance for the csPWS-seg model.
alignments.rationale: The publication revises the methodological claim: instead of focal loss alone being best, a combination including focal loss yielded the best performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automatic and accurate nuclei segmentation offered by the csPWS-seg enhances the reliability of subsequent chromatin analysis research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automatic and accurate nuclei segmentation provided by csPWS-seg enhances the reliability of subsequent chromatin analysis research.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion virtually identically, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The csPWS-seg approach paves the way for more accurate diagnostics, treatment, and understanding of cellular mechanisms for carcinogenesis.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the broader clinical/diagnostic claims about diagnostics and treatment present in the preprint, so this future-applications claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can predict time-varying neural activity evoked by natural sounds with substantially greater accuracy than established models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting model from the study was functionally equivalent to the CNN.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized CNNs outperforming established models; the publication reframes results around model equivalence to CNNs rather than claiming CNN superiority, representing a substantive revision and tempering of the original comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The subspace model was able to predict time-varying spike rate nearly as accurately as the full CNN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A linear–nonlinear subspace model was developed to identify the most informative sensory dimensions captured by a CNN.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes development and purpose of the subspace model (identifying informative dimensions captured by CNNs), clarifying the preprint's performance-focused statement about near-equal prediction accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This analysis revealed a diversity of nonlinear responses consistent with contrast gain control and emergent invariance to spectrotemporal modulation phase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Responses of local neural populations sparsely tiled a shared stimulus subspace.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a related but differently framed observation (sparse tiling of a shared stimulus subspace) rather than the specific nonlinear-response interpretations emphasized in the preprint, so the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Narrow spiking, putative inhibitory neurons showed distinct patterns of tuning that may reflect their position in the cortical circuit.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Encoding properties differed between cell types and layers, reflecting their position in the cortical circuit.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report cell-type and layer-specific encoding differences that relate to circuit position; the publication statement closely matches the preprint's observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results demonstrate a conceptual link between CNN and subspace models and establish a framework for interpretation of deep learning-based models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results establish a framework for interpreting deep-learning-based encoding models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the conceptual link and explicitly states that the results establish an interpretive framework, consistent with the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CelloType adopts a multi-task learning approach that connects the segmentation and classification tasks and simultaneously boosts the performance of both tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CelloType adopts a multitask learning strategy that simultaneously enhances the performance of both segmentation and classification tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's multi-task learning claim with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CelloType outperforms existing segmentation methods using ground-truths from public databases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CelloType outperforms existing segmentation methods on a variety of multiplexed fluorescence and spatial transcriptomic images.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the types of images and settings where CelloType outperforms others, broadening and clarifying the original statement about public-database benchmarks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In terms of classification, CelloType outperforms a baseline model comprised of state-of-the-art methods for individual tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In terms of cell type classification, CelloType surpasses a model composed of state-of-the-art methods for individual tasks and a high-performance instance segmentation model.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the comparison by adding another strong comparator but preserves the core claim that CelloType outperforms baseline composed of state-of-the-art components.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CelloType demonstrates utility for multi-scale segmentation and classification of both cellular and non-cellular elements in a tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CelloType is an end-to-end model designed for cell segmentation and classification in image-based spatial omics data.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a concise contribution statement; the preprint's additional emphasis on multi-scale and non-cellular elements is not as explicit, so the claim is clarified to a more general contribution description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The enhanced accuracy and multi-task-learning ability of CelloType facilitate automated annotation of rapidly growing spatial omics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The enhanced accuracy and multitask learning ability of CelloType facilitate automated annotation of rapidly growing spatial omics data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion essentially verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel generative model emulates chemoaffinity guided axonal growth and dynamically steers the direction of propagation based on distance-dependent chemoattractive forces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel generative model emulates chemoaffinity-guided axonal growth, allowing axons to dynamically steer their direction of propagation based on distance-dependent chemoattractive forces.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model's chemoaffinity-guided, distance-dependent steering mechanism in language very close to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic growth mechanism generates axonal fiber bundles with brain-like geometry and features of complex network architecture consistent with the human brain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This dynamic growth mechanism generates axonal fiber bundles with brain-like geometry and features of complex network architecture consistent with the human brain.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result nearly identically, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model parameters can be fitted to individual connectomes, enabling connectome dimensionality reduction and comparison of parameters between groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model parameters can be fitted to individual connectomes, facilitating connectome dimensionality reduction and comparison of parameters between groups.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same capability for fitting model parameters to individual connectomes for dimensionality reduction and group comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work provides new avenues for understanding neural development from a computational perspective.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work provides new avenues for understanding neural development from a computational perspective by bridging studies of axon guidance and connectome development.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the contribution by explicitly stating it bridges axon guidance and connectome development studies, adding specificity to the preprint's general conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model exhibits characteristics such as lognormally distributed connectivity weights, scale-free nodal degrees, small-worldness, and modularity.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit network-characteristic details (lognormal weights, scale-free degrees, small-worldness, modularity) that were not enumerated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CryoTEN can robustly enhance the quality of cryo-EM density maps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CryoTEN can robustly enhance the quality of cryo-EM density maps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The protein structures built from the density maps processed by CryoTEN have substantially better quality than those built from the original maps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Protein structures built from the density maps processed by CryoTEN have substantially better quality than those built from the original maps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CryoTEN ranks second in improving the quality of density maps compared to existing state-of-the-art deep learning methods, while running more than 10 times faster and requiring much less GPU memory. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CryoTEN ranks second in improving the quality of density maps compared to existing state-of-the-art deep learning methods, while running more than 10 times faster and requiring much less GPU memory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same comparative and performance claims but reports the claim with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GeoDock achieves a 41% success rate for a benchmark set of rigid targets, outperforming all other tested methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GeoDock achieves a 43% top-1 success rate on the Database of Interacting Protein Structures (DIPS) test set, outperforming all other tested methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different numeric value and specifies the benchmark (DIPS), narrowing and revising the original reported rate while keeping the overall outperforming claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For a benchmark set of flexible targets, GeoDock achieves a similar number of top-model successes as the traditional method ClusPro, but fewer than ReplicaDock2. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific flexible-target comparisons to ClusPro or ReplicaDock2, so this preprint claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GeoDock attains an average inference speed of under one second on a single GPU, enabling its application in large-scale structure screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GeoDock attains an average inference speed of under 1�s on a single GPU, enabling its application in large-scale structure screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same timing/result claim with equivalent certainty and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GeoDock is flexible at the protein residue level, allowing the prediction of conformational changes upon binding. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GeoDock is flexible at the protein residue level, allowing the prediction of conformational changes upon binding. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about residue-level flexibility with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture of GeoDock sets up the foundation to capture binding-induced conformational changes, despite current challenges due to limited training and evaluation data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Binding-induced conformational changes are still a challenge owing to limited training and evaluation data. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's optimistic architectural statement into an explicit statement of the remaining challenge, clarifying the limitation while keeping the same focus on data constraints.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNA structure prediction is currently outperformed by traditional methods according to the most recent Critical Assessment of Structure Prediction (CASP15). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNA structure prediction is currently outperformed by traditional methods according to the most recent Critical Assessment of Structure Prediction (CASP15). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this CASP15-based result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNA3DB dataset is designed for training and benchmarking deep learning models by providing a robust way of dividing training, validation, and testing sets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNA3DB dataset is designed for training and benchmarking deep learning models by providing a robust way of dividing training, validation, and testing sets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the dataset-design contribution with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNA3DB ensures that any split of its structurally-dissimilar Components produces test and validation sets that are distinct by sequence and structure from those in the training set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNA3DB ensures that any split of its structurally-dissimilar Components produces test and validation sets that are distinct by sequence and structure from those in the training set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical dataset guarantee unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing literature on RNA structure prediction often reports inflated performance due to using training and testing sets with significant structural overlap. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit critique about inflated performance in prior literature; instead it focuses on providing tools and code, so the critical claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellCycleNet accurately predicts cell cycle phase using only a fluorescent nuclear stain (DAPI) in fixed interphase cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CellCycleNet accurately predicts cell cycle phase using only a fluorescent nuclear stain (DAPI) in fixed interphase cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellCycleNet outperforms state-of-the-art SVM models on each dataset individually. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3D CellCycleNet outperforms support vector machine models on each dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the outperforming claim but reports it with higher certainty and explicitly as 3D CellCycleNet, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When trained on two image datasets simultaneously, CellCycleNet achieves the highest classification accuracy, with an improvement in AUROC of 0.08-0.09. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When trained on two image datasets simultaneously, CellCycleNet achieves the highest classification accuracy (AUROC of 0.94-0.95). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete AUROC values instead of the delta improvement, clarifying the metric while preserving the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates excellent generalization across different microscopes, achieving an AUROC of 0.95. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When trained on two image datasets simultaneously, CellCycleNet achieves the highest classification accuracy (AUROC of 0.94-0.95). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the AUROC under a specific training condition (two datasets) but does not explicitly claim generalization across microscopes; this narrows the original generalization claim to a more specific experimental context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using features derived from 3D images, rather than 2D projections of those same images, significantly improves classification performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using 3D features, rather than 2D features alone, significantly improves classification performance for all model architectures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and broadens the original conclusion by asserting the benefit of 3D features across all model architectures and emphasizes the effect with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAkinet demonstrates generalizability to distinct cell types and organisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAkinet prediction performance generalizes across various cell types and organisms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the generalizability claim but reports it with lower certainty (high -> medium), tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAkinet reproducibly quantifies RNA kinetic parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAkinet can be used to quantify RNA isoform half-lives. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a specific, concrete instantiation of 'kinetic parameters' (isoform half-lives), narrowing the broader preprint claim while maintaining high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAkinet allows the combined interrogation of RNA metabolism and cis-acting RNA regulatory elements. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make a corresponding claim about combined interrogation of metabolism and cis-acting elements, so the preprint's broader conclusion is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing sequence-based domain annotation methods are prone to error, particularly in calling domain boundaries and motifs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing sequence-based domain annotation methods are prone to error in calling domain boundaries and motifs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation with essentially identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dimensionality reduction methods can improve protein domain annotation by taking into account the geometry of protein structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based protein structure prediction can improve sequence-based domain annotation methods by incorporating structural geometry. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes the geometry-aware improvement to deep-learning structure prediction rather than to dimensionality reduction specifically and reports lower certainty, representing a change in the described approach and a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed methods correct mistakes made by existing machine learning-based annotation tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed dimensionality reduction methods can correct mistakes made by existing machine learning-based annotation tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that the methods correct existing tool errors while specifying the methods as dimensionality reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods enable the automated detection of hairpin loops and structural anomalies in the solenoid. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods enable the automated detection of hairpin loops and structural anomalies in the Leucine Rich Repeat solenoid domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the detection claim but specifies the solenoid as the Leucine Rich Repeat domain, clarifying the context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods were validated against a benchmark dataset of 172 manually-annotated LRR domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods were validated against a benchmark dataset of 172 manually-annotated LRR domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Splam is consistently more accurate than SpliceAI, with an overall accuracy of 96% at predicting human splice junctions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Splam achieves 96% accuracy in predicting human splice junctions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 96% accuracy figure for human splice-junction prediction; the core quantitative claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Splam generalizes even to non-human species, including distant ones like the flowering plant Arabidopsis thaliana. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or highlight generalization to non-human species or examples such as Arabidopsis, so this preprint conclusion was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When used to process spliced alignments of RNA-seq data, Splam yields substantial improvements in the accuracy of downstream transcriptome analysis of both poly(A) and ribo-depleted RNA-seq libraries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The downstream-transcriptome-analysis improvement claim is not present in the publication's listed claims and appears to have been dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Splam offers a faster and more accurate approach to detecting splice junctions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compared to SpliceAI, Splam is consistently more accurate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains and emphasizes the accuracy advantage (comparison to SpliceAI) but does not assert or support the preprint's speed claim, narrowing the original combined claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Splam looks at a 400-base-pair window flanking each splice site, reflecting the biological splicing process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a methodological detail (400-bp window) that is not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is projected that 10 million deaths could be attributed to drug-resistant bacteria infections in 2050. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is projected that 10 million deaths could be attributed to drug-resistant bacteria infections in 2050. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The projection is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAMP consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, such as iAMPpred and AMPScanner V2, in terms of accuracy, MCC, G-measure and F1-score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAMP consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, such as iAMPpred and AMPScanner V2, in terms of accuracy, Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC), G-measure, and F1-score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claims and metrics are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAMP captures the residue patterns like sorting signals at around both the N-terminus and the C-terminus, while also retaining the sequence order information from the middle peptide fragments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAMP captures the residue patterns like sorting signals at both the N-terminal and the C-terminal, while also retaining the sequence order information from the middle peptide fragments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic description of SAMP's feature capture is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By leveraging an ensemble RP architecture, SAMP is scalable to processing large-scale AMP identification with further performance improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By leveraging an ensemble RP architecture, SAMP is scalable to processing large-scale AMP identification with further performance improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Scalability and the ensemble RP architecture claim are preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Natural evolutionary information can be used to predictively improve enzyme activity and stability by engineering the active center and protein scaffold. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural evolutionary information can be used to predictively improve enzyme activity and stability by engineering the active center and protein scaffold. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the preprint's core result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The success rate of designed single mutants is approximately 50% in improving either luciferase activity or stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The success rate to improve either luciferase activity or stability of designed single mutants is approximately 50%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported ~50% success rate is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an evolutionary preference in Renilla luciferase towards emitting blue light, which has advantages in terms of water penetration compared to other light spectra. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An evolutionary preference in RLuc toward emitting blue light holds advantages in terms of water penetration compared to other light spectra. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The evolutionary preference and proposed advantage are stated similarly in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach facilitates navigation through enzyme sequence space and offers effective strategies for computer-aided rational enzyme engineering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach facilitates navigation through enzyme sequence space and offers effective strategies for computer-aided rational enzyme engineering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel deep learning-based neural speech decoding framework generates natural-sounding speech and is highly reproducible across a cohort of 48 participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel deep learning-based neural speech decoding framework generates natural-sounding speech and is highly reproducible across a cohort of 48 participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The main result about natural-sounding, reproducible decoding across 48 participants is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Among three neural network architectures for the ECoG Decoder, the 3D ResNet model has the best decoding performance with a PCC of 0.804. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not report the specific architecture comparison or the PCC value, indicating this specific result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models can decode speech with high correlation even when limited to only causal operations, which is necessary for adoption by real-time neural prostheses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models can decode speech with high correlation, even when limited to only causal operations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the causal-operation decoding claim with the same emphasis on high correlation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework successfully decodes speech in participants with either left or right hemisphere coverage, potentially leading to speech prostheses in patients with left hemisphere damage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Speech can be successfully decoded in participants with either left or right hemisphere coverage. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The decoding-in-either-hemisphere point is preserved and stated more strongly in the publication; the prospective prosthesis implication is discussed separately in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework successfully decodes speech in participants with either left or right hemisphere coverage, potentially leading to speech prostheses in patients with left hemisphere damage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This framework could lead to speech prostheses in patients with deficits resulting from left hemisphere damage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the potential translational application for left-hemisphere deficits similarly to the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Open-source code for the two-stage training pipeline and associated tools is provided to enable reproducible research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention provision of open-source code, indicating this contribution was not carried forward in the stated claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) have been successfully used to handle Electronic Health Records (EHR) for predicting conversion from MCI to AD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents two deep learning architectures designed for early predicting conversion from MCI to AD. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe models for predicting MCI→AD conversion; the publication frames this as two deep learning architectures (broader wording) and down-weights certainty, so the claim is clarified with reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed models, PPAD and PPAD-AE, outperformed all baseline models for most prediction scenarios in terms of F2 and sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models, PPAD and PPAD-Autoencoder, outperformed all baseline models for most prediction scenarios in terms of F2 and sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and metrics are the same between preprint and publication (only minor naming expansion), so the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The age feature was one of the top features and was able to address the irregular time interval problem. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The age feature was identified as one of the top features that effectively addressed the irregular time interval problem in electronic health records. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same core claim but with stronger phrasing/certainty, so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ArcheD-predicted Aβ CSF values correlated strongly with measured Aβ CSF values (r =0.81; p <0.001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ArcheD-predicted Aβ CSF values correlated with measured Aβ CSF values and episodic memory measures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the correlation with measured Aβ CSF values and adds correlation with episodic memory, broadening the claim while keeping similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cerebral white matter significantly contributed to CSF prediction in non-symptomatic and early stages of Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cerebral white matter significantly contributed to CSF prediction in non-symptomatic and early stages of Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parietal lobe was the strongest predictor of CSF amyloid levels in those with prodromal or early Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parietal lobe was the strongest predictor of CSF amyloid levels in those with prodromal or early Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with similar certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ArcheD reliably predicted Aβ CSF concentration from Aβ PET scans, offering potential clinical utility for Aβ level determination and early Alzheimer's disease detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ArcheD reliably predicted Aβ CSF concentration from Aβ PET scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core predictive result but omits the preprint's explicit claim about clinical utility/early detection, narrowing the scope of the conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: In late-stage Alzheimer's disease, the brain stem and subcortical areas made more significant contributions to CSF prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result about late-stage contributions appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Not accounting for covariates can lead to suboptimal harmonization. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Not accounting for covariates can lead to suboptimal harmonization. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepResBat and coVAE outperformed ComBat, CovBat, and cVAE in terms of removing dataset differences while enhancing biological effects of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepResBat and coVAE outperformed ComBat, CovBat, and cVAE in terms of removing dataset differences while enhancing biological effects of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over unchanged to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoVAE hallucinates spurious associations between anatomical MRI and covariates even when no association exists. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoVAE hallucinates spurious associations between anatomical MRI and covariates even when no association exists. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepResBat is an effective deep learning alternative to ComBat. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepResBat is an effective deep learning alternative to ComBat. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL-based BSFC has a low false alarm rate of 0.78 events/min with a high Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.943 between detected events and expected events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL-based BSFC has a low false alarm rate of 0.78 events per min with a high Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.943 between detected events and expected events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metrics are identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL-based BSFC of whole blood maintains a detection purity of 72% and a sensitivity of 35.3% for both homotypic and heterotypic CTCCs starting at a minimum size of two cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL-based BSFC of whole blood maintains a detection purity of 72% and a sensitivity of 35.3% for both homotypic and heterotypic CTCCs starting at a minimum size of two cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL-based BSFC is sensitive to changes in the number of CTCCs present in the samples and does not add variability in detection beyond the expected variability from Poisson statistics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL-based BSFC is sensitive to changes in the number of CTCCs present in the samples and does not add variability in detection beyond the expected variability from Poisson statistics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological sensitivity and variability claim is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance established by DL-based BSFC motivates its use for in vivo detection of CTCCs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance established by DL-based BSFC motivates its use for in vivo detection of CTCCs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about motivating in vivo use is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further developments of label-free BSFC to enhance throughput could lead to critical applications in the clinical detection of CTCCs and ex vivo isolation of CTCC from whole blood with minimal disruption and processing steps. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further developments of label-free BSFC to enhance throughput could lead to critical applications in the clinical detection of CTCCs and ex vivo isolation of CTCC from whole blood with minimal disruption and processing steps. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The forward-looking contribution is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current deep learning methods have a limitation in correctly predicting the direction of variant effects on gene expression. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current deep learning methods demonstrate a limitation in correctly predicting the direction of variant effects on gene expression across individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation with a slight emphasis on across-individual prediction; core claim and certainty remain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The limitation in predicting variant effects is due to insufficiently learnt sequence motif grammar. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The limitation in predicting variant effects stems from insufficiently learned sequence motif grammar. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The causal explanation is the same but the publication expresses it with reduced certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New model training strategies are suggested to improve the performance of deep learning methods in predicting gene expression variation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: New model training strategies are suggested to improve the performance of deep learning methods in predicting gene expression variation. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The suggested contribution appears in the publication but with lower expressed certainty, indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN-FEM integration eliminated large errors for out-of-distribution cases in the forward problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of deep neural networks (DNNs) and finite element method (FEM) significantly improves the accuracy of biomechanical analysis compared to DNN-only models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's OOD-forward-error elimination as a more general accuracy improvement versus DNN-only models, changing the comparator and broadening the claim beyond the specific OOD forward-case described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN-FEM integration was magnitudes faster than the FEM-only approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DNN-FEM integration approach is magnitudes faster than the FEM-only method.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance/speed result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN-FEM integration significantly improved performance on the inverse problem with errors less than 1%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DNN-FEM inverse approach reduces parameter identification errors to less than 1%, compared to errors greater than 50% with the FEM-only method.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the <1% inverse-error result but adds a direct comparison to FEM-only (>50% errors), making the claim more specific and emphasizing a larger relative improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The wings of insects are novel structures that evolved independently from legs, attached to the body via a biomechanically complex hinge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The wings of insects are novel structures that evolved independently from legs, unlike those of pterosaurs, birds, and bats.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core evolutionary conclusion but adds an explicit contrast to other flying vertebrates, clarifying the comparative context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional neural network was created that accurately predicts wing motion from the activity of the steering muscles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A convolutional neural network was created that accurately predicts wing motion from the activity of the steering muscles.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same model-performance result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The physics-based simulation incorporating the model of the hinge generates flight maneuvers similar to those of free flying flies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The physics-based simulation incorporating the hinge model generates flight maneuvers that are remarkably similar to those of free-flying flies.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim similar simulated maneuvers; the publication adds emphasis (‘remarkably’) but does not change the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study reveals the mechanical control logic of the insect wing hinge, highlighting its evolutionary significance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study reveals the mechanical control logic of the insect wing hinge, which is among the most sophisticated skeletal structures in the natural world.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and broadens the implication by characterizing the hinge as among the most sophisticated skeletal structures, amplifying the magnitude of the conclusion relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrin expression varied across tissues and cancers, enabling the creation of models that classify samples by tissue or disease status.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrin expression can be used to train machine learning models to distinguish between different healthy tissues and solid tumors with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the utility of integrin expression for classification and makes the performance claim explicit (high accuracy), clarifying rather than contradicting the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ITGA7 was key to classification of breast samples by disease status.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expression of only one or two integrins can classify tissue type, tumor type, or disease status with accuracy greater than 0.9.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's ITGA7-specific finding to a broader statement that one or two integrins suffice with high accuracy; this changes specificity and emphasizes performance, so alignment is plausible but less certain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cancer rewires co-expression for most integrins, but the co-expression relationships of some integrins remain unchanged in healthy and cancer samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrin co-expression networks change significantly from healthy to cancerous breast tissues, indicating changes in functional involvement of integrins due to cancer.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same overall conclusion about significant changes in co-expression, but omits the preprint's caveat about some unchanged relationships, simplifying the framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrin expression in primary breast tumors differed from their metastases, with liver metastasis notably having reduced expression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several integrins such as ITGAD, ITGA4, ITGAL, and ITGA11 have significantly lower expression in metastatic tumors compared to primary tumors.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports specific integrins with lower expression in metastases generally; this shifts the emphasis from the preprint's liver-metastasis observation to a broader set of integrins and metastatic comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning of integrin expression patterns can effectively distinguish samples by tissue or disease status.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrin expression can be used to train machine learning models to distinguish between different healthy tissues and solid tumors with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim restates the preprint's assertion about ML-based discrimination using integrin expression, adding explicit high-accuracy wording but not changing the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based model, Internal Coordinate Net (ICoN), learns the physical principles of conformational changes from Molecular Dynamics simulation data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based model, termed Internal Coordinate Net (ICoN), learns the physical principles of conformational changes from molecular dynamics simulation data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim for ICoN with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model provided a comprehensive sampling of the conformational landscape of the highly dynamic amyloid-β 1-42 monomer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model provided a comprehensive sampling of the conformational landscape of the highly dynamic amyloid-β<sub>1-42</sub> (Aβ42) monomer.
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical claim about sampling Aβ42 conformations; publication adds formatting but not content change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis of synthetic conformations revealed conformational clusters that can be used to rationalize experimental findings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of synthetic conformations revealed conformational clusters that could be used to rationalize experimental findings.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about conformational clusters with equivalent meaning and moderate certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can identify novel conformations with important interactions in atomistic details that are not included in the training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can identify novel conformations with important interactions in atomistic details that are not included in the training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result claim verbatim in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is highly transferable and can be used for any available data for training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work demonstrated the ability of deep learning to utilize natural atomistic motions in protein conformation sampling.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad claim of general transferability is narrowed in the publication to a demonstration of ability to use natural atomistic motions for sampling; the publication frames it as a demonstrated capability, which increases asserted confidence but reduces breadth.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement agents learning at a multitude of timescales possess distinct computational benefits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement agents learning at a multitude of timescales possess distinct computational benefits.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine neurons in mice performing two behavioral tasks encode reward prediction error with a diversity of discount time constants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopaminergic neurons in mice performing two behavioural tasks encode reward prediction error with a diversity of discount time constants.
alignments.rationale: Same empirical finding; differences are orthographic (behavioural/dopaminergic) only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The measured discount factor of individual neurons is correlated across the two tasks, suggesting that it is a cell-specific property.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The measured discount factor of individual neurons is correlated across the two tasks, suggesting that it is a cell-specific property.
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion about correlation of discount factors across tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results provide a new paradigm to understand functional heterogeneity in dopamine neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results provide a new paradigm for understanding functional heterogeneity in dopaminergic neurons.
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim with minor wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings open new avenues for the design of more efficient reinforcement learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings open new avenues for the design of more-efficient reinforcement learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Same forward-looking contribution; only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks are powerful tools for predicting functional genomic regions and have shown success in deciphering gene regulatory logic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All top-performing models utilized neural networks but varied in their architectures and training strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the general claim about neural networks as broadly 'powerful tools' but frames their success specifically in the context of top-performing DREAM models, which narrows the scope of the preprint's general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DREAM Challenge revealed that model architectures and training strategies significantly impact genomics model performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All top-performing models utilized neural networks but varied in their architectures and training strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly notes variation in architectures and training strategies among top models, which clarifies and supports the preprint's claim about the impact of these factors on performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All top-performing models in the DREAM Challenge used neural networks but differed in architectures and training strategies tailored to genomics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All top-performing models utilized neural networks but varied in their architectures and training strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this preprint claim directly and with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models developed during the DREAM Challenge achieved state-of-the-art results on a comprehensive yeast dataset and surpassed existing benchmarks on Drosophila and human genomic datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DREAM Challenge models achieved state-of-the-art results on a comprehensive yeast dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication split the compound preprint claim into separate results; the matched publication claim covers the yeast SOTA portion, so the original combined statement is presented in a narrowed/clarified form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-quality gold-standard genomics datasets can drive significant progress in model development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of gold-standard genomics datasets can drive significant progress in model performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion but with slightly lower certainty (medium vs high), so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Active machine learning can efficiently generate informative training data for predicting the activities of cis-regulatory elements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Active learning is established as an effective strategy for training models of regulatory DNA.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms active learning as an effective strategy for regulatory-DNA modeling, which clarifies and generalizes the preprint's claim about generating informative training data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts the activities of sequences containing binding motifs for the photoreceptor transcription factor Cone-rod homeobox (CRX) using an order of magnitude less training data than current approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific claim about achieving an order-of-magnitude reduction in training data; this performance-efficiency claim is absent from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's internal confidence estimates of its predictions are reliable guides for designing sequences with high activity.
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding statement in the publication about using the model's internal confidence estimates as design guides, so this claim appears to have been dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model correctly identified critical sequence differences between active and inactive sequences with nearly identical transcription factor binding sites.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The iterative training of models on data from multiple rounds of massively parallel reporter assays improves their ability to discriminate between CRX sites with identical sequences but opposite functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports improved discrimination between nearly identical CRX sites, directly matching the preprint's result about identifying critical sequence differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Active learning is established as an effective method to train accurate deep learning models of cis-regulatory function after exhausting naturally occurring training examples in the genome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Active learning is established as an effective strategy for training models of regulatory DNA.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms active learning's effectiveness for regulatory DNA modeling but omits the preprint's specific framing about exhausting natural examples, so the claim is reported in a clarified, slightly less specific form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generative artificial intelligence has the potential to offer novel perspectives in enzyme research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative artificial intelligence has the potential to offer novel perspectives in enzyme research.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's high-level contribution claim but with higher stated certainty (publication marked high vs preprint medium), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of generative models can help discern patterns within the vast sequence space and uncover new functional enzyme sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative models could discern elusive patterns within the vast sequence space and uncover new functional enzyme sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same result but with lower expressed certainty (medium vs preprint's high), so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generative AI can predict mutation effects on enzyme fitness, activity, and stability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction of catalytic activity and stability of enzymes using natural protein sequences indicates how enzyme catalysis shapes enzyme evolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on predicting catalytic activity and stability from natural sequences and linking this to evolution, whereas the preprint more broadly claimed mutation-effect predictions on fitness; the published claim narrows and reframes the original.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction of enzyme activity and stability using natural enzyme sequences indicates how enzyme catalysis shapes enzyme evolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction of catalytic activity and stability of enzymes using natural protein sequences indicates how enzyme catalysis shapes enzyme evolution.
alignments.rationale: This preprint conclusion is carried into the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating generative AI into enzyme studies will enhance our knowledge of enzymes and expedite the creation of superior biocatalysts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of generative AI into enzyme studies will remarkably enhance our knowledge of enzymes and expedite the creation of superior biocatalysts.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the preprint's conclusion about the benefits of integrating generative AI into enzyme research with similar emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of a structure-based machine-learning algorithm with classical molecular dynamics simulations effectively down selects mutations for the rational design of a non-heme iron-dependent lysine dioxygenase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of a structure-based self-supervised machine learning framework with classical molecular dynamics simulations consistently resulted in functional LDO mutants.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution focusing on resulting functional mutants; this clarifies the outcome while preserving the core claim about the combined approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rationally designed single mutants of LDO achieved up to 2-fold higher yields than the wild type (WT).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rationally designed single mutants exhibited up to 2-fold higher expression yields than wild-type (WT) LDO and displayed higher total turnover numbers (TTN).
alignments.rationale: The publication directly reports the same quantitative improvement (up to 2-fold) and expands on additional performance measures, preserving the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pentamutant variant LPNYI LDO exhibits a 40% improvement in total turnover numbers (TTN) compared to WT LDO.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pentamutant variant, LPNYI LDO, shows a 40% improvement in TTN compared to WT LDO.
alignments.rationale: This specific quantitative result is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work provides a low-barrier approach for integrating machine learning algorithms with existing protein engineering strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides a low-barrier approach for integrating machine learning algorithms with existing protein engineering strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this methodological/conceptual claim forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Medical digital twins are computational models of human biology that can be personalized and dynamically updated with patient-specific data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Medical digital twins are computational models of human biology that can be personalized and dynamically updated with patient-specific data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's definitional contribution verbatim and with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamics-informed neural-network controllers are introduced as an alternative approach to control of medical digital twins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in neural-network control methods show promise in addressing complex control problems in biomedical systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution more cautiously as part of broader recent advances and focuses on biomedical control problems rather than explicitly introducing the specific dynamics-informed controllers as an alternative for digital twins.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural-network control method is effective and is benchmarked against other methods using two widely-used agent-based model types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the neural-network control methods is benchmarked against other methods using two widely used ABMs.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the benchmarking against two ABMs and the effectiveness of the proposed controllers, matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relevance of the method extends beyond medical digital twins to other complex dynamical systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in neural-network control methods show promise in addressing complex control problems in biomedical systems.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader claim about applicability to general complex dynamical systems is narrowed in the publication to promise within biomedical systems specifically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BELA is a state-of-the-art ploidy prediction model that surpasses previous image- and video-based models without requiring subjective input from embryologists. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BELA is a state-of-the-art ploidy prediction model that surpasses previous image- and video-based models without necessitating input from embryologists. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication text is effectively the same claim with only minor wording changes ("requiring" -> "necessitating").

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BELA achieves an AUC of 0.76 for discriminating between euploidy and aneuploidy embryos on the Weill Cornell dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BELA achieves an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.76 for discriminating between euploidy and aneuploidy embryos on the Weill Cornell dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and dataset are identical; the publication expands the AUC abbreviation but does not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BELA matches the performance of models trained on embryologists' manual scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BELA matches the performance of models trained on embryologists’ manual scores. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance comparison; only document labeling differs (result vs conclusion).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BELA exemplifies how such models can streamline the embryo evaluation process, reducing time and effort required by embryologists. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BELA exemplifies how such models can streamline the embryo evaluation process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the streamlining claim but omits the explicit "reducing time and effort" wording, slightly narrowing the scope of the asserted benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'Detect Any Mouse Model' (DAMM) is a pretrained object detector that localizes mice in complex environments with robust performance and minimal additional training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'Detect-Any-Mouse-Model' (DAMM) effectively localizes mice in complex environments with minimal training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core contribution (localizing mice with minimal training); wording and emphasis are essentially the same.
