alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMBER-designed models match or even exceed published expert-designed models in accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AMBER's predictions match or even exceed published expert-designed models.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative accuracy against expert-designed models as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMBER can accurately discover functional genomic variants in allele-specific binding and disease heritability enrichment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AMBER accurately discovers functional genomic variants in allele-specific binding and disease heritability enrichment.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result but with higher reported certainty (publication labels this result high confidence), indicating amplified confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMBER utilizes the full space of computational operations for accurately modelling genomic sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim that AMBER utilizes the full space of computational operations; this broader methodological claim from the preprint is not present in the published claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Administrations of cancer-specific neoepitopes in the form of synthetic peptide vaccine have been proven effective in both mouse models and human patients.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim asserts or repeats the preprint's assertion about proven efficacy of neoepitope peptide vaccines in mouse models and human patients, so this claim was not carried into the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selection of potent, immunogenic neoepitopes remains a challenging step in cancer vaccine development.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this methodological limitation; no matching cautionary claim is present in the published claims, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MHCSeqNet competes favorably with state-of-the-art predictors and exhibits promising generalization to new MHC class I and class II alleles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MHCSeqNet outperforms state-of-the-art predictors on both MHC binding affinity and MHC ligand peptidome datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a stronger performance claim ("outperforms" on benchmark datasets) relative to the preprint's "competes favorably"; the dataset/performance focus is retained though the generalization to class II alleles is not explicitly stated in this matched publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MHCSeqNet employs neural network architectures developed for natural language processing to model amino acid sequence representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MHCSeqNet employs neural network architectures developed for natural language processing to model amino acid sequence representations.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated verbatim in the publication with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The flexibility offered by MHCSeqNet should make it a valuable tool for screening effective neoepitopes in cancer vaccine development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improved performance and flexibility of MHCSeqNet make it a valuable tool for screening effective neoepitopes in cancer vaccine development.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the conclusion more definitive and ties it explicitly to improved performance and flexibility, increasing confidence compared with the preprint's conditional phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ENNGene simplifies the training of custom CNN or hybrid CNN-RNN models on genomic data through an easy-to-use Graphical User Interface.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ENNGene simplifies the training of custom CNN or hybrid CNN-RNN models on genomic data via an easy-to-use Graphical User Interface.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution nearly verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ENNGene allows users to fully customize the network architecture, including the number and types of layers and each layer's precise set-up.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ENNGene allows users to fully customize the network architecture, including the number and types of layers and each layer's precise set-up.
alignments.rationale: The claim about architecture customization is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using ENNGene, multiple models trained on the RBP24 dataset quickly reached state-of-the-art performance while improving results for more than half of the proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using ENNGene, multiple models trained on the RBP24 dataset quickly reached state-of-the-art performance while improving results for more than half of the proteins.
alignments.rationale: The reported benchmarking result is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deployment of Integrated Gradients in ENNGene provides users with a graphical representation of the attribution level of each input position.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ENNGene provides Integrated Gradients to facilitate interpretation of predicted results by offering a graphical representation of attribution levels for each input position.
alignments.rationale: The published wording slightly reorganizes phrasing but conveys the same contribution (Integrated Gradients and graphical attribution), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classic, non-deep models can achieve good prediction accuracy with much smaller datasets than previously thought.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can achieve good prediction accuracy with much smaller datasets than previously thought.
alignments.rationale: The publication reverses the subject from "classic, non-deep models" to "deep learning," representing a substantive revision of which model class achieves good accuracy, and the published claim is stated with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks further improve performance with the same amount of data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks can finely discriminate between input DNA sequences.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's performance-improvement claim is presented in the publication as an explanation of CNNs' discriminative ability; this reframes performance improvement as finer sequence discrimination rather than an explicit comparative performance statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks have an improved ability to discriminate between input sequences and extract sequence features that are highly predictive of protein expression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks can finely discriminate between input DNA sequences.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert CNNs' improved discriminative ability on sequence inputs; the publication's phrasing is slightly more concise but equivalent in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Controlled sequence diversity leads to important gains in data efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Controlled sequence diversity leads to substantial gains in data efficiency.
alignments.rationale: This result is restated in the publication with synonymous wording (important -> substantial) and no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide practitioners with guidelines for designing experimental screens that strike a balance between cost and quality of training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide guidelines for designing genotype-phenotype screens that balance cost and quality of training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the practical-conclusion content, tailoring the application wording to genotype-phenotype screens but keeping the same guidance and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepBend consistently performs on par with alternative machine learning and deep learning models while providing mechanistic interpretations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepBend consistently performs on par with alternative models while providing mechanistic interpretations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance and interpretability claim with equivalent meaning, omitting an explicit enumeration of 'machine learning and deep learning' but keeping the intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepBend confirmed known motifs of DNA bendability and revealed several novel motifs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepBend confirmed known motifs of DNA bendability and revealed several novel motifs.
alignments.rationale: The motif-confirmation and discovery claim is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial patterns of motif occurrences influence DNA bendability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial patterns of motif occurrences influence DNA bendability.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this mechanistic conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepBend's genome-wide prediction of bendability is linked to chromatin conformation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepBend's genome-wide prediction of bendability is linked to chromatin conformation.
alignments.rationale: The link between predicted bendability and chromatin conformation appears in the publication with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepBend revealed the motifs controlling bendability of topologically associated domains and their boundaries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepBend revealed the motifs controlling the bendability of topologically associated domains and their boundaries.
alignments.rationale: The specific finding about motifs controlling TAD bendability is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine tuning the RoseTTAFold structure prediction network on protein structure denoising tasks results in a generative model of protein backbones.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning the RoseTTAFold structure prediction network on protein structure denoising tasks results in a generative model of protein backbones with outstanding performance on various protein design challenges.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the fine-tuning->generative-model claim but strengthens it by explicitly asserting outstanding performance on downstream design challenges, increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RoseTTAFold Diffusion method achieves outstanding performance on various protein design tasks, including monomer design and enzyme active site scaffolding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning the RoseTTAFold structure prediction network on protein structure denoising tasks results in a generative model of protein backbones with outstanding performance on various protein design challenges.
alignments.rationale: The publication consolidates and affirms the preprint's performance claim about RFdiffusion/RoseTTAFold diffusion achieving outstanding results on multiple protein design tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RF diffusion enables the design of diverse, complex, functional proteins from simple molecular specifications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method called RoseTTAFold diffusion (RFdiffusion) demonstrates the ability to design diverse functional proteins from simple molecular specifications.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that RFdiffusion can design diverse functional proteins from simple specifications but omits explicit mention of "complex," slightly narrowing the scope of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first model, NU-ResNet, effectively encodes RNA sequence-structure information into a 3D matrix. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop two deep learning models for evaluating RNA sequence-secondary structure pairs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the two models more generally (evaluating sequence-structure pairs) and omits the specific technical detail about encoding into a 3D matrix, so the claim is reformulated/clarified and somewhat narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second model, NUMO-ResNet, incorporates additional information from RNA characterizations, specifically 2D folding motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop two deep learning models for evaluating RNA sequence-secondary structure pairs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication groups the two models together without the specific mention of 2D folding motifs, so the technical detail is reduced (clarified/narrowed) though the contribution remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed models demonstrate excellent performance across two independent testing datasets and surpass the performance of the ENTRNA approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed models show superior performance compared to the models from literatures when being tested across different independent RNA families. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements claim superior performance across independent datasets/families; the publication wording generalizes to multiple literature models but preserves the core performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is currently no consensus on the most effective features for characterizing RNA sequence-structure pairs. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or discuss a lack of consensus about features; this limitation present in the preprint is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a method, called Shutter Island, that uses a deep learning model to detect genomic islands. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, Shutter Island, uses a deep learning model to detect genomic islands. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim; no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate that this image-based approach generalizes better than the existing tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The image-based approach of Shutter Island generalizes better than existing tools for detecting genomic islands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about improved generalization with essentially identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep neural network was retrained on only a limited number of GI datasets and then successfully generalized. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network was re-trained on a limited number of GI datasets and successfully generalized to make novel GI predictions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the outcome (novel GI predictions) and frames the claim as a result, but retains the core claim about retraining on limited data and successful generalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach could be applied to other problems in the field where data is still lacking or hard to curate. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach used in Shutter Island could be applied to other problems in the field where data is still lacking or hard to curate. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same generalization/applicability claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: siRNADesign achieves state-of-the-art performance in multiple internal datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: siRNADiscovery achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple internal datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims the same state-of-the-art performance on multiple internal datasets; only the tool name is slightly different.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: siRNADesign outperforms existing methodologies in in vitro wet lab experiments and an externally validated dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: siRNADiscovery outperforms existing methodologies in in vitro studies and on an externally validated dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental and external validation outperforming claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: siRNADesign has demonstrated remarkable predictive accuracy and robustness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: siRNADiscovery has demonstrated remarkable predictive accuracy and robustness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim mirrors the preprint regarding predictive accuracy and robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new data-splitting methodology developed addresses the data leakage issue, ensuring the robustness and stability of the model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new data-splitting methodology developed addresses the data leakage issue, ensuring the robustness and stability of the model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological contribution claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach in redefining data-splitting standards aims to set new benchmarks for future research in predictive biological modeling for siRNA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach to redefining data-splitting standards aims to set new benchmarks for future research in predictive biological modeling for siRNA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the forward-looking contribution about setting new benchmarks with the data-splitting approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of artificial intelligence into microscopy systems significantly enhances performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of artificial intelligence into microscopy systems significantly enhances performance, optimizing both image acquisition and analysis phases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on where performance is enhanced (acquisition and analysis), clarifying and slightly elaborating the original high-level conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Development of AI-assisted super-resolution microscopy is often limited by access to large biological datasets and benchmarking difficulties. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this limitation statement; the preprint's comment about dataset/benchmarking constraints is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pySTED simulation platform demonstrates benefits for the development and deployment of AI strategies in super-resolution microscopy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the benefits of a realistic stimulated emission depletion microscopy simulation platform, pySTED, for the development and deployment of artificial intelligence strategies for super-resolution microscopy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about pySTED's benefits for AI development and deployment in super-resolution microscopy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulation environment provided by pySTED allows for the augmentation of data for training deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the benefits of a realistic stimulated emission depletion microscopy simulation platform, pySTED, for the development and deployment of artificial intelligence strategies for super-resolution microscopy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim about pySTED benefits encompasses data augmentation for training networks, so the core result is preserved though expressed more generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning models can be successfully trained and deployed on a real microscope using the pySTED platform. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using pySTED as a training environment allows the reinforcement learning models to bridge the gap between simulation and reality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim as bridging simulation and reality, clarifying the mechanism/outcome of training RL models with pySTED and preserving the successful sim-to-real message.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Particle-based reaction-diffusion models can simulate explicit self-assembly of particles into higher-order structures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scalability of parallel NERDSS is evaluated for reversible reactions and demonstrates accurate solutions for higher-order self-assembly in 3D and 2D. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly demonstrates accurate solutions for higher-order self-assembly, which aligns closely with the preprint's claim about simulating explicit self-assembly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parallel implementation of the NERDSS software achieves close to linear scaling for up to 96 processors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parallel implementation of the NERDSS software achieves close to linear scaling for up to 96 processors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this precise performance result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Parallel efficiency of NERDSS depends on system size, reaction network, and limiting timescales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Parallel efficiency of NERDSS depends on system size, timescales, and reaction network, showing optimal scaling for smaller assemblies with slower timescales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds detail about where scaling is optimal (smaller assemblies, slower timescales), which clarifies and narrows the general dependency statement from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The formation of very large assemblies poses challenges in evaluating reaction updates across processors. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Parallel efficiency of NERDSS depends on system size, timescales, and reaction network, showing optimal scaling for smaller assemblies with slower timescales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the limitation as explicitly but implies scaling issues for larger assemblies by specifying optimal conditions; thus the limitation was reframed/clarified rather than repeated verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parallel NERDSS code is provided as open source with detailed documentation for developers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parallel NERDSS code is provided as open-source, facilitating development and extension to other particle-based reaction-diffusion software. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the open-source availability but shifts emphasis toward facilitation and extension; the certainty label is slightly lower in the publication, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating single-cell RNA sequencing with artificial intelligence can lead to advanced therapeutic discoveries. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not contain or reiterate this broad translational claim about advanced therapeutic discoveries; it focuses on the model and its properties rather than claiming direct therapeutic breakthroughs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scGFT model is a train-free, cell-centric generative model that synthesizes single cells with natural gene expression profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scGFT is a train-free, cell-centric generative model that synthesizes single cells with natural gene expression profiles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical description of scGFT verbatim as a core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scGFT has been validated to synthesize cells that preserve the intrinsic characteristics found in scRNA-seq data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scGFT demonstrates mathematical rigor and validates its ability to synthesize cells that preserve intrinsic characteristics of scRNA-seq data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the validation claim but adds emphasis on mathematical rigor, i.e., a clarification/strengthening of methods detail rather than a contradiction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scGFT provides a scalable solution to overcome data scarcity in the context of AI-driven precision medicine. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scGFT provides a scalable solution to mitigate data scarcity in cell-targeted research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the scalable/data-scarcity claim but frames the application domain more narrowly (cell-targeted research vs broader AI-driven precision medicine).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-quality, comprehensive datasets are necessary to train machine learning models in computational proteomics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-quality, comprehensive data sets are necessary to train machine learning models in computational proteomics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Larger datasets typically yield more accurate machine learning models in proteomics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger data sets typically yield more accurate machine learning models in proteomics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Algorithmic strategies such as self-supervised pretraining and multi-task learning can help address data scarcity in proteomics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Algorithmic strategies such as self-supervised pretraining and multitask learning can help address data scarcity in proteomics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological suggestion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Standardization of data is essential for robust model development in machine learning applications in proteomics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Standardization of data is essential for robust model development in machine learning applications in proteomics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This practical conclusion appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning-driven high-throughput method was developed for the discovery of novel repellent molecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning-driven high-throughput method was developed for the discovery of novel repellent molecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution claim as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The graph neural network (GNN) approach outperformed a chemoinformatic model and produced a hit rate that increased with training data size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph neural network (GNN) approach outperformed a chemoinformatic model and produced a hit rate that increased with training data size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance and data-dependence finding for the GNN is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More than 10 molecules with repellency similar to or greater than the most widely used repellents were identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More than 10 molecules with repellency similar to or greater than the most widely used repellents were identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported number and potency of identified molecules is repeated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was a limited correlation between the neural responses from the mosquito antennal lobe to selected repellents and the GNN representation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strong neural responses were found in the mosquito antennal lobe to many of the tested compounds, including those predicted to be strong repellents. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized limited correlation between neural responses and the model representation, whereas the publication highlights strong neural responses to many compounds; this changes the reported relationship and thus revises the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enables computational screening of billions of possible molecules to identify candidate repellents. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach enables computational screening of billions of possible molecules to identify empirically tractable numbers of candidate repellents. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the conclusion by specifying that screening yields empirically tractable candidate sets, a clarification of the original computational claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GEO-BERT has demonstrated optimal performance on multiple benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GEO-BERT has demonstrated optimal performance on multiple benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the benchmark-performance claim as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two potent and novel DYRK1A inhibitors (IC 50 : <1 μM) were ultimately discovered at a hit rate of 10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two potent and novel DYRK1A inhibitors (IC<sub>50</sub>: <1 μM) were ultimately discovered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the discovery of two inhibitors but omits the explicit 10% hit-rate figure reported in the preprint, a clarifying (less quantitative) change in reporting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed the Geometry-based BERT model for molecular property prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have developed an open-source GEO-BERT model for molecular property prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the detail that the model is open-source; the core contribution (GEO-BERT) remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GEO-BERT improves the accuracy of property prediction by using three-dimensional structural information within the molecule. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GEO-BERT enhances its ability to characterize molecular structures by introducing three different positional relationships: atom-atom, bond-bond, and atom-bond. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the mechanism (three positional relationships) by which 3D information is incorporated, clarifying the originally stated improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A triple mutant ACE2 variant, named ACE2 <sub>2</sub> .v.2.4, exhibits nanomolar affinity binding to the RBD domain of the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A triple mutant variant of ACE2, named sACE2<sub>2</sub>.v.2.4, exhibits subnanomolar affinity to the receptor-binding domain of the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the quantitative binding claim from 'nanomolar' to 'subnanomolar' affinity, a revision that amplifies the reported binding strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several ACE2 double mutants were identified that bind to the RBD with tighter affinity compared to the wild type. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TLmutation model identified multiple ACE2 double mutants that bind SARS-CoV-2 spike protein with tighter affinity compared to the wild type. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result about double mutants binding tighter than wild type, with similar substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The double mutant L79V;N90D binds to the RBD with similar affinity to ACE2 <sub>2</sub> .v.2.4. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The double mutant L79V;N90D binds the receptor-binding domain of SARS-CoV-2 spike protein similarly to ACE2<sub>2</sub>.v.2.4. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific comparative binding result is presented the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The successful experimental validation of the double mutants demonstrates the effectiveness of transfer and supervised learning approaches for engineering protein-protein interactions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental validation of the double mutants demonstrates the effectiveness of machine learning approaches for engineering protein-protein interactions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the methodological claim from specific 'transfer and supervised learning' approaches to machine learning approaches more broadly, broadening the scope of the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate a workflow and computer vision model for automatically categorizing species color pattern from community science images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate a workflow and computer vision model for automatically categorizing species color pattern from community science images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim text are effectively identical in content and emphasis, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model was highly accurate (∼98%) despite image heterogeneity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model was highly accurate (∼98%) despite image heterogeneity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result statement is the same in both versions with the same reported accuracy and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We documented extensive niche overlap between morphs, but wider niche breadth for striped morphs at the range-wide scale. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We documented extensive niche overlap between morphs, but wider niche breadth for striped morphs at the range-wide scale. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The ecological result is restated verbatim, indicating no change in semantic content or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work showcases key design principles for using machine learning with heterogeneous community science image data to address questions at an unprecedented scale. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work showcases key design principles for using machine learning with heterogeneous community science image data to address questions at an unprecedented scale. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication expresses stronger certainty (medium -> high) and frames it as a contribution, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine receptor activity has a bidirectional modulatory effect on the level of exploration, where increasing dopamine activity decreases exploration and decreasing dopamine activity increases exploration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing dopamine activity decreased exploration and decreasing dopamine activity increased exploration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the bidirectional effect of dopamine on exploration with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Beta-noradrenergic receptor activity also modulated exploration, but this effect was mediated by sex. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modulatory effect of beta-noradrenergic receptor activity on exploration was mediated by sex. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The mediated effect by sex is preserved verbatim between versions with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine modulation affects exploration via decision noise, while norepinephrine modulation affects exploration via outcome sensitivity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine modulation affected exploration via decision noise and norepinephrine modulation affected exploration via sensitivity to outcome. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic claim is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mechanisms governing the transition between exploration and exploitation are sensitive to changes in both catecholamine functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mechanisms that govern the exploration-exploitation transition are sensitive to changes in both catecholamine functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim appears unchanged in content, scope, and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of deep generative models can accelerate the early stages of drug development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The general claim about accelerating early drug development is not present in the publication's listed claims and appears omitted or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The latent diffusion model, DrugDiff, successfully generates unique, diverse, and novel small molecules with targeted properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained latent diffusion model, DrugDiff, successfully generates unique, diverse, and novel small molecules with targeted properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core result about DrugDiff's generative performance with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture of DrugDiff is designed to be highly flexible and easily adaptable to future scenarios. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework demonstrated in this study is highly flexible, allowing for the easy addition or removal of molecular properties without retraining the model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific description of the flexibility (add/remove properties without retraining), refining the preprint's broader statement about adaptability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work expands the use of generative modeling in drug development to include small molecules, which are more complex to model than proteins and RNA. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly frames the work as expanding generative modeling to small molecules, a claim not present in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of deep generative models to small molecule generation addresses a new level of difficulty compared to sequence-based molecule generation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about the particular difficulty of small-molecule generative modeling relative to sequence-based cases, which was not in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoBeL-RL is a closed-loop simulator of complex behavior and learning based on reinforcement learning and deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoBeL-RL is a closed-loop simulator of complex behavior and learning based on reinforcement learning and deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoBeL-RL provides a neuroscience-oriented framework for efficiently setting up and running simulations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoBeL-RL provides a neuroscience-oriented framework for efficiently setting up and running simulations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions of human navigation behaviors in virtual reality can be made with high accuracy using the proposed model across different subjects and tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An artificial agent based on the modular model can reproduce complex human navigation trajectories in novel environments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An artificial agent based on the modular model can reproduce complex human navigation trajectories in novel environments. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reliably predicted instantaneous heart period from whole brain fMRI data both within and across individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models reliably predicted instantaneous heart period from whole-brain fMRI data both within and across individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prediction accuracies were highest when measured within-participants. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A network of cortical and subcortical brain regions linked to psychological stress were reliable predictors of variation in heart period. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A network of cortical and subcortical brain regions linked to visceral motor and sensory processes were reliable predictors of variation in heart period. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research adds to evidence on brain-heart interactions and contributes to developing clinically-applicable biomarkers of brain contributions to cardiovascular disease risk. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research constitutes an incremental step toward developing clinically applicable biomarkers of brain contributions to cardiovascular disease risk. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altered chemosensory function at the level of the carotid bodies and/or the nucleus tractus solitarii are responsible for the blunted response to hypoxia in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=hypothesis)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered chemosensory function at the level of the carotid bodies and/or the nucleus tractus solitarii is hypothesized to be responsible for the blunted response to hypoxia in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=hypothesis)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Oxygen carrying capacity is the most salient factor for producing silent hypoxemia. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinicians are called to measure hematocrit as a clinical index of altered physiology in response to COVID-19 infection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fixational eye movements enhance the precision of the retinal representation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fixational eye movements enhance the precision of the retinal representation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the main conclusion about fixational eye movements with identical framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed Bayesian method for reconstructing natural images from retinal spikes matched or surpassed the performance of previous reconstruction algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the specific claim that the Bayesian reconstruction method matched or surpassed prior algorithms, so this algorithm-performance claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reconstructions were improved with artificial stimulus jitter that emulated fixational eye movements. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reconstructions were improved with artificial stimulus jitter that emulated fixational eye movements. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reconstructions were degraded by small artificial perturbations of spike times, revealing more precise temporal encoding than suggested by previous studies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reconstructions were degraded by small artificial perturbations of spike times, revealing more precise temporal encoding than suggested by previous studies. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reconstructions were substantially degraded when derived from a model that ignored cell-to-cell interactions, indicating the importance of stimulus-evoked correlations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reconstructions were substantially degraded when derived from a model that ignored cell-to-cell interactions, indicating the importance of stimulus-evoked correlations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Any dose-adjustment is a strong negative predictor of successful loading of dofetilide. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Any dose-adjustment during loading and a history of coronary artery disease are strong negative predictors of successful dofetilide loading. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Dose adjustments are a strong determinant of whether patients are able to successfully initiate dofetilide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple supervised approaches failed to identify any model that predicted dose adjustments better than a naïve approach. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A reinforcement-learning algorithm predicted patient characteristics and dosing decisions that resulted in the lowest risk of failure to be discharged on dofetilide. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning approaches have the potential to help solve phylogenetic problems with greater accuracy and computational efficiency. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches have the potential to help solve phylogenetic problems with greater accuracy and computational efficiency. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning methods can exhibit superior performance in some difficult regions of branch length parameter space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning methods can exhibit superior performance in some difficult regions of branch length parameter space. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning methods are more efficient and accurate when inferring long branches associated with distantly related taxa compared to maximum likelihood inference. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning methods are more efficient and accurate compared to maximum likelihood inference for estimating branch lengths. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE) exhibit atypical functional topographies compared to healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TLE-related topographic changes reflect reductions in hierarchical flow patterns between cortical systems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings of functional perturbations in TLE are independent of TLE-related cortical grey matter atrophy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The magnitude of functional perturbations in TLE is robustly associated with behavioral markers of memory function. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new encoding scheme developed in CRIP is superior to existing feature representation methods for RNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed steerable, dithered, single-objective tilted light sheet reduces fluorescence background in single-molecule super-resolution imaging. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The sequences generated by megaDNA demonstrated consistent differences in various compositional metrics when compared to natural bacteriophage genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The reconstruction method based on adaptive kernel regression resolves conformational states to a higher resolution than all other tested methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RECOVAR allows for better interpretability of the latent space by identifying stable states and low free-energy motions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PCA embedding allows for accurate estimation of conformational density, improving interpretability by identifying stable states and low free-energy motions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication clarifies the mechanism (PCA embedding and accurate density estimation) that underlies interpretability, refining the preprint's interpretability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The code for RECOVAR is made freely available for public use. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's explicit claim about making code freely available is absent from the publication's listed claims, indicating removal of that assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Normal aging leads to myelin alterations in the rhesus monkey dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, which are correlated with cognitive impairment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Normal aging leads to myelin alterations in the rhesus monkey dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, which are positively correlated with degree of cognitive impairment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding with nearly identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our models predict that biologically plausible levels of myelin dystrophy can account for substantial working memory impairment with aging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models predict that biologically plausible levels of myelin dystrophy, if uncompensated by other factors, can account for substantial working memory impairment with aging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the conditional caveat 'if uncompensated by other factors,' narrowing the claim and tempering its implicational strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Complete remyelination nearly recovers axonal transmission and network function to unperturbed levels. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complete remyelination nearly recovered axonal transmission and network function to unperturbed levels. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim about remyelination restoring function is preserved with similar certainty; wording tense/labeling changed but meaning maintained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinctive parameter sets likely modulate an axon's susceptibility to conduction velocity changes following demyelination versus remyelination. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific mechanistic claim about parameter sets modulating susceptibility does not appear among the publication's listed claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The present computational study has broader implications for many demyelinating conditions, such as multiple sclerosis or schizophrenia. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The present computational study unites empirical data from ultrastructure up to behavior during normal aging. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the preprint's speculative broader implications for disease with a focused claim about uniting empirical aging data; the contribution is reframed and stated with higher confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The formation of long-lived intermediates or kinetic traps can dramatically reduce the yield of functional products during self-assembly of macromolecules.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the negative observation about kinetic traps reducing yield; it focuses on solutions rather than reiterating this problem, so the preprint claim is effectively not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two biologically-inspired protocols can prevent kinetic trapping through internal design of subunit binding kinetics or external design of subunit titration in time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-yield complexes can be steered by internal design of rate-constants or external control of subunits to efficiently assemble.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same two strategies (internal vs external control) for steering assembly toward high yield with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preventative solutions via interface design are the most efficient and scale better with more subunits compared to external control via titration or recycling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Internal design of a hierarchy of subunit binding rates allows self-assembly to robustly avoid kinetic traps for all concentrations and energetics.
alignments.rationale: The published claim shifts emphasis from an efficiency/scale comparison to a stronger, more general statement about robustness of internal rate-hierarchy design across concentrations/energetics, changing the original comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diverse subunits help access more efficient solutions when following external control protocols and simplify design for internal control.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The kinetics of self-assembly benefits from diversity in subunits, improving the expressivity of self-assembly.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert that subunit diversity benefits assembly; the publication frames it as improved expressivity but conveys the same point about the value of diversity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results identify universal scaling in the cost of kinetic trapping and provide multiple strategies for eliminating trapping and maximizing assembly yield.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state a claim about 'universal scaling in the cost of kinetic trapping'; instead it focuses on strategies to avoid traps, so this specific universal-scaling claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a general preference for A/T-ending codons in the Saccharomycotina subphylum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Codon usage bias within the Saccharomycotina is shaped by genomic features and GC bias, but additional forces may also be influencing the evolution of specific codons.
alignments.rationale: The publication links codon preferences to GC bias and genomic features and explicitly adds caveats about other forces, which clarifies and slightly tempers the preprint's categorical A/T-preference statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Codon usage bias can classify yeasts into orders with an accuracy greater than 90% using a machine learning algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Codon usage bias is distinct between the 12 orders of Saccharomycotina, allowing yeasts to be classified with an accuracy greater than 90% using a machine learning algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The published claim restates the classification result and the >90% accuracy using ML, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The degree of translational selection is influenced by genome features and assembly metrics, including the number of coding sequences, BUSCO count, and genome length.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Codon usage bias is influenced by a combination of features, including the number of coding sequences, BUSCO count, and genome length.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same set of genomic/assembly features influencing codon bias, matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extreme avoidance of CGN codons in Saccharomycodales is associated with a decline in arginine tRNA function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An extreme bias in codon usage in the Saccharomycodales is associated with a lack of predicted arginine tRNAs that decode CGN codons.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same association between extreme CGN avoidance and impaired/predicted absence of arginine tRNA decoding, consistent with the preprint's observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Codon usage bias within the Saccharomycotina is shaped by both genomic features and GC bias, with some cases of extreme preference and avoidance along yeast lineages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Codon usage bias within the Saccharomycotina is shaped by genomic features and GC bias, but additional forces may also be influencing the evolution of specific codons.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core conclusion about genomic and GC-bias influences but adds explicit caveats about other forces, clarifying and slightly tempering the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TERRACE is a new algorithm for full-length assembly of circRNAs from paired-end total RNA-seq data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TERRACE is a new algorithm for full-length assembly of circRNAs from paired-end total RNA-seq data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TERRACE consistently outperforms leading circRNA detection methods in sensitivity while maintaining better or comparable precision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TERRACE consistently outperforms existing methods by a large margin in sensitivity while achieving better or comparable precision.
alignments.rationale: The published result claims the same consistent sensitivity gains and similar precision, matching the preprint's performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When annotations are not provided, TERRACE can assemble 123%-412% more correct circRNAs than state-of-the-art methods on human tissues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When annotations are not provided, TERRACE assembles 123%-413% more correct circRNAs than state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numerical improvement (a one-percentage-point difference in the upper bound) and the same context, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TERRACE incorporates a new machine-learning approach that assigns a confidence score to each assembled circRNA, which is superior to using abundance for scoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TERRACE incorporates a new machine-learning approach trained to assign a confidence score to each assembled circRNA, which is shown to be superior to using abundance for scoring.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication describe the same ML-based confidence scoring and claim it outperforms abundance as a score.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models can be used for estimating the age of otoliths from images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models can be used for estimating the age of otoliths from images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core claim that DL models can estimate otolith age from images without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's precision is comparable to and may even surpass that of human experts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's precision is comparable to documented precision obtained by human experts.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert comparable precision to human experts; the publication frames it relative to documented expert precision but preserves the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automating the analysis of otolith images will help to improve consistency, lower cost, and increase the scale of age prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automating the analysis of otolith images may help to improve consistency, lower cost, and increase the extent of age estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the benefits claimed for automation (consistency, cost, scale/extent), using slightly more cautious wording but not changing the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method represents an important step forward for improving the age structure estimates of fish populations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method could be used to estimate the age of other species using images of otoliths or fish scales, given adequate data are available.
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the potential applicability beyond the originally stated improvement for fish-population age-structure to other species and sample types, making the claim more general.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational framework integrates mathematical programming and graph neural networks to classify entire pathways under various conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational framework integrates mathematical programming and Graph Neural Networks to classify entire pathways under various conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim (minor capitalization differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for the identification of mutations with distinguishable functional profiles related to specific phenotypes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for the identification of mutations with distinguishable functional profiles related to specific phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The published claim restates the same result about identifying mutations with distinct functional profiles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This framework provides a data-driven pipeline for genotype-to-phenotype translation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This framework provides a data-driven pipeline for genotype-to-phenotype translation.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same high-level conclusion about genotype-to-phenotype translation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scalable approach enables the classification of diverse conditions and reveals new functional patterns through a causal representation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scalable approach enables the classification of diverse conditions and reveals new functional patterns through a causal representation.
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint's description of scalability and discovery of new functional patterns using a causal representation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HOLIgraph achieved a median balanced accuracy of over 90 percent when predicting inhibitors for OATP1B1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HOLIgraph achieved a median balanced accuracy of over 90 percent when predicting inhibitors for OATP1B1. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HOLIgraph outperforms traditional DDI prediction models which rely solely on ligand molecular features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HOLIgraph outperforms traditional DDI prediction models which rely solely on ligand molecular features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data used to train HOLIgraph can enable the characterization of protein residues involved in inhibitory drug-OATP interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data used to train HOLIgraph can enable the characterization of protein residues involved in inhibitory drug-OATP interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain OATP1B1 residues that preferentially interact with inhibitors, including I46 and K49, were identified. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain OATP1B1 residues that preferentially interact with inhibitors, including I46 and K49, were identified. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HOLIgraph introduces a new paradigm for DDI prediction by incorporating protein-ligand interactions derived from docking simulations into a graph neural net framework. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed transformer architecture provides a very early prediction of the correct movement direction no later than 230ms after the Go signal presentation across animals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture provides an early prediction of the correct movement direction, achieving accurate results no later than 230 ms after the Go signal presentation across animals. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The internal dynamics of the model show that the predicted correlations between time steps and between neurons at successive layers evolve in a manner that mirrors previous theoretical analyses. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolution of predicted correlations between time steps and between neurons at successive layers of the architecture mirrors findings from previous theoretical analyses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework provides a comprehensive use case for the practical implementation of deep learning tools in motor control research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework provides a comprehensive use case for the practical implementation of deep learning tools in motor control research. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAdLSA shows significant improvement over established sequence alignment methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAdLSA shows significant improvement over established sequence alignment methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAdLSA can detect subtle, yet significant, structural relationships between multiple protein sequences using the same deep-learning neural network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAdLSA can detect subtle, yet significant, structural relationships between multiple protein sequences using the same deep-learning neural network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can be used to unlock greater insights from reef soundscapes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can be used to unlock greater insights from reef soundscapes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supervised learning can train models that identify ecological classes and individual sites from whole soundscapes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised learning can be used to train models that can identify ecological classes and individual sites from whole soundscapes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Unsupervised clustering achieves a more detailed understanding of ecological and site groupings within soundscape data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pretrained convolutional neural network (P-CNN) is a powerful tool for identifying marine soundscape ecologists due to its strong performance and low computational cost. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pretrained convolutional neural network (P-CNN) offers a powerful tool for generating insights from marine soundscape data while requiring significantly less computational resources. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The groupings produced by unsupervised machine learning hold relationships with ground truth ecological data, including coral coverage, fish community, and depth. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The T-CNN performs marginally better across tasks compared to the P-CNN, but the P-CNN achieves near comparable performance with significant improvements over acoustic indices. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning features inferred from digital pathology scans can improve the validity and robustness of current clinico-pathological features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning features inferred from digital pathology scans can improve the validity and robustness of current clinico-pathological features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DAPPER framework is introduced as a first framework to evaluate deep features and classifiers in digital pathology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DAPPER framework is based on a rigorous data analysis plan derived from the FDA’s MAQC project. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DAPPER framework is introduced for validation based on a rigorous Data Analysis Plan derived from the FDA's MAQC project. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the need for random features and random labels diagnostic tests to identify selection bias and risks for reproducibility. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning can be used to maintain populations at target levels in co-cultures within continuous bioreactors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-free performance with bang-bang control can outperform a traditional proportional integral controller with continuous control when faced with infrequent sampling. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A satisfactory control policy can be learned in one twenty-four hour experiment by running five bioreactors in parallel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A satisfactory control policy can be learned in one twenty-four hour experiment by running five bioreactors in parallel. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-training on tasks such as masked symbol prediction and next sentence prediction yields higher performance on downstream tasks compared to no pre-training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pre-training on tasks such as masked symbol prediction and next sentence prediction yields higher performance on downstream tasks compared to no pre-training. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The larger base model does not outperform the smaller medium model. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PPML-Omics could protect patients' privacy.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the privacy-preserving intent but phrases it more cautiously as "could protect" patients' privacy, tempering the original strong design claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PPML-Omics outperformed comparison methods under the same level of privacy guarantee in various omic data tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPML-Omics was able to outperform methods of comparison under the same level of privacy guarantee.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical performance claim against comparison methods under equivalent privacy guarantees without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PPML-Omics provides a mathematically guaranteed method with robust and generalizable empirical performance in protecting patients' privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPML-Omics is the first mathematically guaranteed method with robust and generalizable empirical performance in protecting patients' privacy in omic data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a novelty claim ("the first") and presents the statement somewhat more cautiously, so the core claim is clarified and positioned as novel while the confidence tone is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method addresses privacy concerns in cancer classification, clustering, and integration of spatial gene expression and tumor morphology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPML-Omics balances the privacy-preserving capability and utility in omic data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's task-specific privacy claims into a broader balance between privacy and utility across omic analyses, a reframing that softens the specific task-level assertions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-throughput technologies are important for discovering prognostic molecular signatures and identifying novel drug targets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-throughput technologies are effective in discovering prognostic biomarkers and identifying novel drug targets in breast cancer.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion but narrows its domain to breast cancer specifically, clarifying the context of the statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stability of feature selection and reproducibility of identified gene sets can be improved by including information on molecular networks in machine learning methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including information on molecular networks in machine learning methods improves the stability and interpretability of selected features.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same idea and explicitly links network information to both stability and interpretability, slightly clarifying the outcome without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Graph Convolutional Neural Network (GCNN) is a contemporary deep learning approach that can be applied to gene expression data structured by a prior knowledge molecular network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GCNN+LRP delivers the most stable and interpretable gene lists compared to GCNN+SHAP and classical ML-based feature selection approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from a general methodological contribution about GCNN applicability to a specific empirical claim that a GCNN+LRP pipeline outperforms alternatives, which revises the nature of the original methodological claim into a performance result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) are effective techniques to explain individual decisions of deep learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GCNN+LRP delivers the most stable and interpretable gene lists compared to GCNN+SHAP and classical ML-based feature selection approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides an empirical comparison showing LRP (in GCNN+LRP) yields the most stable and interpretable lists versus SHAP, narrowing the general claim to a specific comparative result and strengthening the relative effectiveness of LRP.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study analyzed the stability, impact on classification performance, and interpretability of selected feature sets using both GCNN-based and classical ML-based feature selection approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GCNN+LRP delivers the most stable and interpretable gene lists compared to GCNN+SHAP and classical ML-based feature selection approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the study's comparative analysis results directly, clarifying that GCNN+LRP produced the most stable and interpretable lists among the evaluated approaches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HiCDiffusion model produces high-resolution Hi-C matrices that better resemble experimental results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HiCDiffusion model produces high-resolution Hi-C matrices that better resemble experimental results.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this primary result verbatim; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Fréchet inception distance improves by an average of 12 times with the HiCDiffusion model, with the highest improvement of 35 times.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Fréchet inception distance is improved by an average of 11 times with the HiCDiffusion model, with the highest improvement of 56 times.
alignments.rationale: The core metric improvement claim is preserved but the reported numerical factors differ between preprint and publication, representing a revision to the quantitative details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HiCDiffusion model maintains similar classic metrics to current state-of-the-art encoder-decoder architectures used for predicting chromatin interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HiCDiffusion model maintains similar classic metrics to current state-of-the-art encoder-decoder architectures.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparative performance statement with no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ViraMiner achieves significantly improved accuracy compared to other machine learning methods for viral genome classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ViraMiner achieves significantly improved accuracy compared to other machine learning methods for viral genome classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the main performance claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using 300 bp contigs, ViraMiner achieves 0.923 area under the ROC curve.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using 300 bp contigs, ViraMiner achieves 0.923 area under the ROC curve.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first machine learning methodology that can detect the presence of viral sequences among raw metagenomic contigs from diverse human samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first machine learning methodology that can detect the presence of viral sequences among raw metagenomic contigs from diverse human samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the novelty/contribution claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model captures different types of information of genome composition and can be used as a recommendation system to further investigate sequences labeled as 'unknown' by conventional alignment methods.
