alignments.preprint_claim_text: MFmap enables the analysis of cell-state transformations between different cancer subtypes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MFmap enables the analysis of cell-state transformations between different cancer subtypes.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MFmap provides a new method for selecting appropriate cell line models that closely resemble specific tumor types.
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not include a claim about selecting cell line models; this contribution is omitted in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MFmap is a semi-supervised generative model that achieves good generative and predictive performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MFmap is a semi-supervised method that achieves good generative and predictive performance.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same semi-supervised generative/predictive performance without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HetSIREN can fully reconstruct or refine a CryoEM volume in real space based on the structural information summarized in a conformational latent space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HetSIREN can refine images’ pose while conditioning the network with additional constraints to yield cleaner high-quality volumes.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes pose refinement conditioned with constraints rather than the broader claim of full reconstruction from conformational latent space, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HetSIREN introduces a novel encoding architecture able to decouple pose and CTF information from the conformational landscape, resulting in more accurate and interpretable conformational latent spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HetSIREN addresses the issue of entanglement between structural heterogeneity estimations and pose estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as resolving entanglement between heterogeneity and pose estimation; mention of CTF decoupling is absent, slightly narrowing and tempering the original scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An important finding is the sensitivity of the structure and dynamics of the SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein on the storage temperature.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding biological finding about SARS-CoV-2 Spike temperature sensitivity appears among the publication claims, so this result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new learning method called e-prop approaches the performance of BPTT for training recurrent neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The e-prop learning method approaches the performance of backpropagation through time (BPTT) for training recurrent neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of experimental data and mathematical insight enables biologically plausible online network learning through gradient descent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of two pieces of experimental data enables biologically plausible online network learning through gradient descent.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the explanatory basis (specifying two experimental data pieces rather than experimental data plus mathematical insight), modifying the basis of the claim while keeping the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-prop suggests a method for powerful on-chip learning in novel energy-efficient spike-based hardware for AI.
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-prop suggests a method for powerful on-chip learning in energy-efficient spike-based hardware for artificial intelligence.
alignments.rationale: The contribution and scope are preserved with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VoroIF-GNN is a novel single-model method for assessing inter-subunit interfaces in protein-protein complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VoroIF-GNN is a novel method for assessing inter-subunit interfaces in protein-protein complexes using only the input structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the detail that the method uses only the input structure, clarifying but not changing the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VoroIF-GNN demonstrates strong performance in selecting the best multimeric model during CASP15.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VoroIF-GNN demonstrates strong performance in selecting the best multimeric model during CASP15.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method produces whole interface-level scores by summarizing contact-level predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method predicts the accuracy of interface contacts using an attention-based graph neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that contact-level accuracy is predicted with an attention-based GNN; this clarifies the mechanism underlying the contact-level predictions and their aggregation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The toolkit fibertools enables fast and accurate identification of m6A-marked bases using PacBio single-molecule long-read sequencing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The toolkit fibertools enables fast and accurate identification of m6A-marked bases using single-molecule long-read sequencing.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution; the mention of PacBio is dropped but the claim remains the same in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fibertools achieves over 90% precision and recall in DNA-m6A identification along DNA molecules longer than 20 kilobases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: fibertools achieves over 90% precision and recall in DNA-m6A identification along DNA molecules longer than 20 kb.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fibertools provides a ~1,000-fold improvement in speed for DNA-m6A identification compared to previous methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: fibertools provides an approximately 1000-fold improvement in speed for DNA-m6A identification.
alignments.rationale: The performance improvement claim is preserved with equivalent magnitude and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fibertools can integrate genetic and epigenetic data at single-molecule resolution, facilitating accurate analyses in variable genomic regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: fibertools can integrate genetic and epigenetic data at single-molecule resolution, facilitating accurate analyses in variable genomic regions.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about integrative single-molecule analyses is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Mask R-CNN-based tracker can segment C. elegans dauers and S. carpocapsae infective juveniles on a textured background suitable for nictation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Mask R-CNN-based tracker can segment C. elegans dauers and S. carpocapsae infective juveniles on a textured background suitable for nictation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical segmentation claim verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nictation propensity of C. elegans from high-density liquid cultures largely mirrors their development into dauers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nictation propensity of C. elegans from high-density liquid cultures largely mirrors their development into dauers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same observational claim and level of certainty without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning pipeline can score nictation behavior in S. carpocapsae infective juveniles in the presence of a potential host. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system quantifies nictation in S. carpocapsae infective juveniles in the presence of a potential host. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This system improves upon existing intensity-based tracking algorithms and human scoring, facilitating large-scale studies of nictation and potentially other nematode behaviors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This system improves upon existing intensity-based tracking algorithms and human scoring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-driven data analysis revealed a pattern of KLRG1 expression that uniquely identified intratumoral effector CD4 T cell populations associated with tumor burden across tumor models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-driven data analysis revealed a pattern of KLRG1 expression that uniquely identified intratumoral effector CD4 T cell populations associated with tumor burden across tumor models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same ML-derived identification claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Helios - KLRG1 + subset of tumor-infiltrating regulatory T cells was associated with tumor progression from immune equilibrium to escape and was lost in tumors responding to immune checkpoint blockade. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Helios<sup>-</sup>KLRG1<sup>+</sup> subset of tumor-infiltrating regulatory T cells was associated with tumor progression from immune equilibrium to escape and was lost in tumors responding to ICB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same association and context, using standard abbreviation (ICB) but not altering the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validation studies confirmed that KLRG1 signatures in human tumor-infiltrating CD4 T cells associate with disease progression in renal cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validation studies confirmed KLRG1 signatures in human tumor-infiltrating CD4 T cells associate with disease progression in renal cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validated association in human renal cancer is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings nominate KLRG1 + CD4 T cell populations as subsets for further investigation in cancer immunity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings nominate KLRG1<sup>+</sup> CD4 T cell populations as subsets for further investigation in cancer immunity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same nominative conclusion about KLRG1+ CD4 T cell subsets with the same level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the utility of longitudinal spectral flow profiling as an engine of dynamic biomarker and/or target discovery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the utility of longitudinal spectral flow profiling as an engine of dynamic biomarker discovery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the explicit "and/or target" language, narrowing the stated utility to biomarker discovery while retaining the overall contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HRMAn is an open-source image analysis platform based on machine learning algorithms and deep learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HRMAn is an open-source image analysis platform based on machine learning algorithms and deep learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about HRMAn being an open-source ML/deep learning image analysis platform.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HRMAn has the capability to learn phenotypes from the data, without relying on researcher-based assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HRMAn has the capacity to learn phenotypes from the data, without relying on researcher-based assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that HRMAn can learn phenotypes from data without researcher bias.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HRMAn can recognize, classify, and quantify pathogen killing, replication, and cellular defense responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HRMAn can recognize, classify, and quantify pathogen killing, replication, and cellular defense responses using Toxoplasma gondii and Salmonella enterica Typhimurium. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific pathogen examples demonstrating the capability, which clarifies and narrows the original general claim to demonstrated organisms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The OrthoRep system durably hypermutates chosen genes at a rate of >10^-4 substitutions per base in vivo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Upgraded orthogonal DNA replication (OrthoRep) systems radically accelerate the evolution of chosen genes under selection in yeast. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific measured hypermutation rate into a broader, stronger claim about radically accelerating gene evolution under selection, dropping the explicit numeric rate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using OrthoRep to evolve a maladapted enzyme resulted in thousands of unique multi-mutation sequences with many pairs >60 amino acids apart, revealing factors influencing enzyme adaptation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of OrthoRep to a maladapted biosynthetic enzyme resulted in collections of extensively diverged sequences that revealed structural and environmental constraints shaping the enzyme's activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same experimental outcome while specifying the types of constraints (structural and environmental), clarifying the nature of the revealed factors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fitness of evolved sequences was not predictable by advanced machine learning models trained on natural variation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat or address the claim about predictability by ML models, indicating it was omitted from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OrthoRep supports the prospective and systematic discovery of constraints shaping gene evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The upgraded OrthoRep systems should support the discovery of factors influencing gene evolution and uncover previously unknown regions of fitness landscapes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the conclusion to include uncovering unknown fitness landscape regions but hedges with "should," which tempers the confidence relative to the preprint's more direct support claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OrthoRep uncovers new regions in fitness landscapes and has general applications in biomolecular engineering. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OrthoRep systems have potential for broad applications in biomolecular engineering. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the notion of broad applications but couches it as potential, softening the assertiveness compared with the preprint's phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Protein Transformer Variational AutoEncoder (ProT-VAE) provides an accurate and transferable model of the sequence-function relationship for data-driven protein engineering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Protein Transformer Variational AutoEncoder (ProT-VAE) is an accurate, generative, fast, and transferable model for data-driven protein design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the characterization (adding "generative" and "fast") which amplifies the original claim while retaining accuracy and transferability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProT-VAE facilitates understanding, optimization, and generative design of functional synthetic sequences through interpretable low-dimensional latent spaces. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Protein Transformer Variational AutoEncoder (ProT-VAE) is an accurate, generative, fast, and transferable model for data-driven protein design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes generative and performance attributes but omits the specific claim about interpretable low-dimensional latent spaces, shifting emphasis away from interpretability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ProT-VAE latent space reveals ancestral and functional relationships that enable the conditional generation of novel sequences with high functionality and substantial sequence diversity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ProT-VAE model identifies a phenylalanine hydroxylase enzyme with 2.5× catalytic activity over wild-type. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies concrete experimental demonstrations of generated functional sequences (e.g., PH enzyme with 2.5× activity), narrowing the general latent-space claim to specific validated outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can serve as a generic platform for machine learning-guided directed evolution campaigns aimed at designing novel synthetic proteins with enhanced functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Protein Transformer Variational AutoEncoder (ProT-VAE) is an accurate, generative, fast, and transferable model for data-driven protein design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the model more strongly (adding performance qualifiers) and presents it as a transferable design tool, which amplifies the original claim about serving as a generic platform.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decoding accuracy was significantly above chance level when the classifier was trained on IFG activity and tested on future activity in superior and middle temporal gyrus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decoding accuracy significantly exceeded chance level when the classifier was trained on IFG activity and tested on STG/MTG activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent statistical claim and brain regions; wording is the same in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results present new evidence for a model predictive speech comprehension where predictive IFG activity is fed back to shape subsequent activity in STG/MTG. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inferior frontal gyrus (IFG) activity influences subsequent activity in superior/middle temporal gyri (STG/MTG) during speech comprehension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same causal/feedback relationship more succinctly as an influence during comprehension; the core claim is preserved but slightly reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings provide evidence of dynamic feedback and feedforward influences between IFG and temporal areas during N400 generation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings indicate a dynamic flow of information between IFG and temporal areas in generating semantic predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dynamic flow between IFG and temporal areas but frames it in terms of semantic predictions and lowers certainty (medium), so the claim is clarified and its confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: V4 and inferotemporal (IT) visual category-selective units discriminated fixations on targets and distractors during the search, but were differentially engaged by face and house targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: V4 and inferotemporal (IT) visual category-selective units discriminated fixations on targets and distractors during the search but were differentially engaged by face and house targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental result with identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fixations on targets reduced spike-LFP coherence within the temporal cortex compared to distractors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fixations on targets reduced spike-LFP coherence within the temporal cortex compared with distractors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated verbatim in the publication with no change in meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Target-induced desynchronization between the temporal and prefrontal cortices was only evident for face targets, suggesting that attention to faces differentially engaged the prefrontal cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Target-induced desynchronization between the temporal and prefrontal cortices was only evident for face targets, suggesting that attention to faces differentially engaged the prefrontal cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion and confidence in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The search became more efficient with increasing target-induced desynchronization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The search became more efficient with increasing target-induced desynchronization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest domain specificity for attending to faces and an intricate interplay between visual attention and social processing neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest domain specificity for attending to faces and an intricate interplay between visual attention and social processing neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
