alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutations in enhancers more commonly result in loss of function (60%) than in gain of function (9%). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieved the highest efficiency retron-Eco1 editrons to date for human genome editing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved the highest efficiency Retron-Eco1 editrons to date for human genome editing. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Long-read sequencing technology enables highly accurate detection of allele-specific RNA expression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Long-read sequencing technology enables highly accurate detection of allele-specific RNA expression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analyses reveal the importance of sequences adjacent to the DRACH motif in determining m6A deposition. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This technological advance will further our understanding of the role of genetics in determining mRNA modifications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study revealed a novel three-state kinetic mechanism in the CRISPR/Cas9 system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Energy landscape analysis reveals a novel three-state mechanism and identifies key nucleotides essential for gene editing. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The CrossLabFit methodology integrates data from multiple laboratories to overcome the constraints of single-lab data collection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution; only minor renaming/wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach harmonizes disparate qualitative assessments into a unified framework for parameter estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach harmonizes disparate qualitative assessments into a unified framework for parameter estimation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Content is the same; publication changes the label of the claim but not its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of machine learning algorithms allows qualitative constraints to be represented as dynamic 'qualitative windows' that capture significant trends. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of machine learning clustering allows qualitative constraints to be represented as dynamic 'feasible windows' that capture significant trends. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies 'clustering' (a more specific ML approach) and renames the concept to 'feasible windows', which clarifies methodology/terminology without changing substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GPU-accelerated version of differential evolution improves the navigation in the cost function that integrates quantitative and qualitative data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GPU-accelerated version of differential evolution improves the navigation of the cost function that integrates quantitative and qualitative information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same technical result with only minor wording adjustments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology demonstrates significant improvements in model accuracy and parameter identifiability across a series of case studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation of the approach across case studies demonstrates significant improvements in model accuracy and parameter identifiability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication frames this as validation but the substantive claim (improvements shown across case studies) is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed framework achieves an acquisition speed of 200 volumes per second with high resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieves high-speed imaging at 200 vol/s with a lateral resolution of up to 5.02 ± 0.54 μm and an axial resolution of 9.02 ± 1.11 μm.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the 200 vol/s result but adds specific lateral and axial resolution numbers, clarifying the previously vague 'high resolution' claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system allows for real-time volumetric imaging and assessment of contractile dynamics at cellular resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental and computational framework developed allows for real-time volumetric tracking of cardiac contractility at single-cell resolution.
alignments.rationale: Wording and substance are effectively identical: both claim real-time volumetric tracking/assessment of cardiac contractility at single-cell/cellular resolution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strategy provides significant potential for exploring intercellular interactions in both health and disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework supports in-depth investigation of intercellular interactions in health and disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about enabling investigation of intercellular interactions in health and disease with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced morphology-appropriate representation learning framework uses 3D rotation invariant autoencoders and point clouds to learn representations of complex multi-piece morphologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed morphology-appropriate representation learning framework uses three-dimensional rotation-invariant autoencoders and point clouds to learn representations of complex shapes that are independent of orientation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution with equivalent wording (rotation-invariant 3D autoencoders + point clouds) and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our framework facilitates the unsupervised discovery of sub-clusters for each intracellular structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can facilitate the unsupervised discovery of subclusters for intracellular structures with complex morphologies.
alignments.rationale: Both statements claim the same unsupervised subcluster discovery capability for complex intracellular morphologies with equivalent strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can be applied to phenotypic profiling using a dataset of nucleolar images following drug perturbations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach can be applied to phenotypic profiling using a dataset of nucleolar images following drug perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same application claim (phenotypic profiling on nucleolar images post-drug perturbation) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We systematically compare our framework to image-based autoencoders across several intracellular structure datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed framework is benchmarked against image-based autoencoders across efficiency, generative capability, and representation expressivity metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the benchmarking metrics (efficiency, generative capability, expressivity) rather than the broader phrasing 'across several datasets', which clarifies and narrows the nature of the systematic comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChronoRoot combines 3D printed open-hardware with deep segmentation networks for high temporal resolution phenotyping of plant roots.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of machine intelligence methods and a 3D-printed device expands the possibilities of root high-throughput phenotyping for genetics and natural variation studies.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe combining 3D-printed hardware with computational methods; the publication frames the outcome more in terms of expanded possibilities and specific applications, clarifying the original contribution though not repeating the explicit 'high temporal resolution' wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel deep learning based root extraction method was developed that incorporates temporal consistency into the root system architecture reconstruction process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a novel deep learning-based root extraction method that leverages the latest advances in convolutional neural networks for image segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms a novel deep-learning extraction method but emphasizes CNN advances and omits explicit mention of temporal-consistency integration, altering the emphasis of the methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic extraction of phenotypic parameters from sequences of images allows for a comprehensive characterization of root system growth dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic extraction of phenotypic parameters from sequences of images allowed a comprehensive characterization of the root system growth dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about automatic extraction enabling comprehensive characterization of growth dynamics with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of machine intelligence methods and a 3D-printed device expands the possibilities of root high-throughput phenotyping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of machine intelligence methods and a 3D-printed device expands the possibilities of root high-throughput phenotyping for genetics and natural variation studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies particular application domains (genetics and natural variation studies), clarifying the original broad claim but narrowing its immediate framing to those research areas.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hedgehog diffused extra-cellularly as a monomer and rapidly transitioned between membrane-confined and -unconfined states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hedgehog diffused extracellularly as a monomer and rapidly transitioned between membrane-confined and -unconfined states.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the experimental result verbatim: monomeric extracellular diffusion with rapid transitions between confined and unconfined membrane states.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The vertebrate-specific protein SCUBE1 expanded Hedgehog gradients by accelerating the transition rates between states without affecting the relative abundance of molecules in each state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The vertebrate-specific protein SCUBE1 expanded Hedgehog gradients by accelerating the transition rates between states without affecting the relative abundance of molecules in each state.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same mechanistic result regarding SCUBE1's effect on Hedgehog gradient expansion without changing molecule-state abundances.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A topology-limited diffusion model was developed in which cell-cell gaps create diffusion barriers, and morphogens can only overcome the barrier by passing through a membrane-unconfined state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A topology-limited diffusion model was developed in which cell-cell gaps create diffusion barriers that morphogens can only overcome by passing through a membrane-unconfined state.
alignments.rationale: The model claim is the same in both versions, but the publication lists this contribution with reduced certainty (medium vs. preprint's high), so the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SCUBE1 promotes Hedgehog secretion and diffusion by allowing it to transiently overcome diffusion barriers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SCUBE1 promoted Hedgehog secretion and diffusion by allowing it to transiently overcome diffusion barriers.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same mechanistic role for SCUBE1 in promoting Hedgehog secretion and transiently overcoming diffusion barriers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This multiscale understanding of morphogen gradient formation unified prior models and discovered novel knobs that nature can use to tune morphogen gradient sizes across tissues and organisms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This multiscale understanding of morphogen gradient formation unified prior models and identified knobs that nature can use to tune morphogen gradient sizes across tissues and organisms.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim unification of models and identification of tunable parameters ('knobs'); wording differences are minor and do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Network spread models captured the spatiotemporal evolution of tau and amyloid in human subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Network spread models captured the spatiotemporal evolution of tau and amyloid in human subjects.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about network spread models capturing tau and amyloid evolution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model including a one-way amyloid-to-tau aggregation interaction performed best.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model including a one-way amyloid-to-tau aggregation interaction performed best.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the identical claim that the one-way amyloid-to-tau aggregation interaction model performed best.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides quantitative, whole-brain support to the concept of amyloid-facilitated-tauopathy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides quantitative, whole-brain support to the concept of amyloid-facilitated-tauopathy.
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is the same, but the publication indicates a lower certainty level (medium) than the preprint (high), so confidence is classified as tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings help explain certain known but poorly understood aspects of Alzheimer's disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings help explain certain known but poorly understood aspects of Alzheimer's disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same interpretive conclusion about explaining poorly understood aspects of Alzheimer's disease with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeePathNet integrates cancer-specific pathway information into multi-omic data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeePathNet integrates cancer-specific pathway information into multiomic data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with only minor wording changes (multi-omic vs multiomic), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeePathNet outperforms traditional methods for predicting drug response and classifying cancer type and subtype. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeePathNet outperforms traditional methods for predicting drug response and classifying cancer type and subtype. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeePathNet enables biomarker discovery at the pathway level. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By enabling pathway-level biomarker discovery, DeePathNet represents a significant advancement in cancer research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the pathway-level biomarker discovery claim but adds evaluative language about its significance, clarifying and strengthening the framing (hence amplified confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeePathNet utilizes Transformer-based deep learning for superior accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention Transformer-based architecture or specifically claim superior accuracy due to Transformers, so this architecture-specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LncRNAs can be accurately predicted from genomic sequences with the highest accuracy of 83.4% for Z. mays and the lowest accuracy of 57.9% for B. rapa. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LncRNAs can be accurately predicted from genomic sequences with the highest accuracy of 83.4% for Zea mays and the lowest accuracy of 57.9% for Brassica rapa. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the numeric accuracy results with only taxonomic name expansion; the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of lncRNA identification may be affected by genome assembly quality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of lncRNA identification may be affected by genome assembly quality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The caution about assembly quality is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NLP models can achieve an average accuracy of 63.1% for cross-species prediction using target species not previously seen by the model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP models can achieve an average accuracy of 63.1% for cross-species prediction using target species not previously seen by the model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The cross-species average accuracy result is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models can be interpreted using explainable artificial intelligence to identify motifs important to lncRNA prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models can be interpreted using explainable artificial intelligence to identify motifs important to lncRNA prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Interpretability via XAI to identify motifs is present unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identification of lncRNAs from genomic sequences allows for the detection of lowly expressed lncRNAs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat the specific claim about enabling detection of lowly expressed lncRNAs, so this conclusion appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Holotomographic microscopy (HTM) offers unprecedented opportunities to observe mitochondrial network dynamics in endothelial cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Holotomographic microscopy (HTM) enables real-time, label-free visualization of endothelial cells and their organelles, such as mitochondria. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts wording to emphasize real-time, label-free visualization; this clarifies the practical capability described in the preprint without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning model using a U-net learner with a Resnet18 encoder can accurately identify mitochondrial networks, cell borders, endothelial cells, and background in HTM images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning model using a U-net learner with a Resnet18 encoder can accurately identify mitochondrial networks, cell borders, endothelial cells, and background within HTM images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this methodological/result claim with only minor wording adjustments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method provides high accuracy and similarity metrics for visualizing mitochondrial networks within HTM images of endothelial cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed method provides high accuracy and similarity metrics for visualizing mitochondrial networks within HTM images of endothelial cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach holds promise in advancing the understanding of cardiovascular disease mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach holds promise in advancing the understanding of cardiovascular disease mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding translational potential is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed empirical graph neural network (EGNA) provides accurate predictions of protein-ligand binding affinity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed empirical graph neural network (EGNA) enables accurate protein-ligand binding affinity prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the accuracy claim for EGNA's binding affinity prediction; wording difference (provides vs enables) does not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EGNA effectively captures interaction patterns by simulating empirical scoring functions through graph convolutional layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EGNA captures interaction patterns precisely by simulating empirical scoring functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The mechanism claim is maintained in the publication with minor wording changes; core claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EGNA demonstrates superiority over state-of-the-art machine learning-based scoring functions on benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EGNA demonstrates superiority over state-of-the-art machine learning-based scoring functions on benchmark data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The superiority on benchmarks is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The contributions of different factors on binding affinity can be transparently investigated using EGNA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contributions of different factors on binding affinity can be transparently investigated using EGNA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretability/transparency claim is present unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We integrated over 25,000 mass spectrometry experiments using a machine learning approach to identify over 15,000 human protein complexes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We integrated over 25,000 mass spectrometry experiments to identify more than 15,000 human protein complexes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the experimental scale and complex count but omits explicit mention of a machine learning approach, altering the methodological attribution (hence revised).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our map of protein complexes is highly accurate and more comprehensive than previous maps, placing approximately 75% of human proteins into their physical contexts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our map of protein complexes is highly accurate and more comprehensive than previous maps, placing nearly 70% of human proteins into their physical contexts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower coverage estimate (≈70% vs ≈75%), so the claim has been revised and narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify co-varying complexes suggesting common functional associations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about co-varying complexes implying functional associations is not present in the publication's listed claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify expression as the primary way cells and organisms relieve the conflict of mutually exclusive subunits. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify expression as the primary way cells and organisms relieve the conflict of mutually exclusive subunits. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This biological conclusion is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We expect our resource to be highly impactful to the broader research community. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We expect our resource to be highly impactful to the broader research community. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the expectation of impact but raises the stated certainty from low to medium, clarifying and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNA nanotechnology enables the construction of designer nanoscale structures with high programmability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNA nanotechnology enables the construction of designer nanoscale structures with high programmability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication statement is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The properties and structure of DNA constructs are greatly altered in vivo due to spontaneous protein adsorption from biofluids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The properties and structure of DNA constructs are greatly altered in vivo due to spontaneous protein adsorption from biofluids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated verbatim in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified protein characteristics important for their adsorption to DNA nanostructures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified protein characteristics important for their adsorption to DNA nanostructures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication presents the finding with higher certainty (medium -> high), so semantic content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a machine-learning model that predicts which proteins will be enriched on a DNA nanostructure based on the DNA structures' design features and protein properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning model was developed that predicts which proteins will be enriched on a DNA nanostructure based on the DNA structures' design features and protein properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model development and predictive scope with equivalent certainty and detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work will help to understand and program the function of DNA nanostructures in vivo for biophysical and biomedical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work will help to understand and program the function of DNA nanostructures in vivo for biophysical and biomedical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is carried over verbatim with the same scope and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatially distinct lipids had higher overall degree of unsaturated fatty acids in distal lung regions compared to proximal regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are differences in the abundance of spatially distinct lipids in the lung following ozone exposure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports spatial differences in lipid distribution more generally, omitting the specific finding about unsaturation degree in distal vs proximal regions; content is therefore revised and slightly narrowed in specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The airway and alveolar epithelium exhibited significantly decreased sphingolipid and glycerophospholipid abundance in females, but not in males. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights sex differences in regional lung lipid distribution after ozone exposure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the overall message of sex differences but drops the detailed lipid-class and sex-specific phrasing, reducing specificity and lowering expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the potential role of lipid saturation in healthy lung function and highlight sex differences in regional lung lipid distribution following ozone exposure. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights sex differences in regional lung lipid distribution after ozone exposure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the sex-differences element but omits the broader claim about lipid saturation's role in healthy lung function, so the original conclusion is partially revised and presented with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study provides a framework for future MSI experiments capable of relative quantification across biological replicates and expansion to multiple sample types, including human tissue. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research provides a framework for future mass spectrometry imaging experiments capable of relative quantification across biological replicates. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same methodological framework more confidently but omits the explicit mention of expansion to multiple sample types (e.g., human), thus clarifying and narrowing the original contribution while increasing stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Advancements in genotyping allow for early diagnosis of cardiomyopathies, potentially enabling earlier treatment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Advancements in genotyping allow for early diagnosis of cardiomyopathies, potentially enabling earlier treatment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the high-level conclusion verbatim; content, scope and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A spatially explicit half sarcomere muscle model was developed to estimate therapeutic targets for mutations in mouse muscle. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A spatially explicit half sarcomere muscle model was developed to estimate therapeutic targets for mutations in mouse muscle. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried forward without change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) was trained using a large dataset of simulated isometric twitches to predict rate parameters associated with muscle contractions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A conditional variational autoencoder was trained using a large data set of simulated isometric twitches to predict rate parameters associated with muscle function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methods and predictive aim are the same; wording is slightly different but semantic content and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts rate parameters associated with twitches from control mice with the cardiac Troponin C (cTnC) I61Q variant and those treated with the myosin activator Danicamtiv. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model predicts rate parameters that can recover abnormal twitch responses associated with the cardiac troponin C (cTnC) I61Q variant. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the model's ability to recover abnormal responses for the I61Q variant but omits mention of predictions for Danicamtiv-treated mice, narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration-segregation difference (ISD) captures both global efficiency and clustering in functional brain networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration-segregation difference (ISD) captures two key network properties: network efficiency and clustering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution in equivalent terms (efficiency and clustering), preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Changes in ISD indicate a profound shift to segregation in both whole brain and all brain subnetworks during anesthesia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed changes in ISD suggest a profound shift towards the segregation of brain networks during anesthesia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding is maintained but the publication expresses it with lower certainty (high -> medium), so the semantic content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random forest machine learning models identified awake vs. unresponsive states and their transitions with accuracy up to 93%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models using integration and segregation data accurately identify awake vs. unresponsive states and their transitions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the models' accurate classification but omits the explicit accuracy figure (93%), removing a quantitative claim and slightly tempering the claim's specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metastability is more effectively explained by integration, while complexity is more closely linked with segregation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Metastability is more effectively explained by integration, while complexity is more closely linked with segregation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this interpretive conclusion verbatim with the same expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration-segregation balance is a useful index that can differentiate among various conscious and unconscious states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ISD reliably indexes states of consciousness. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same conclusion more directly (ISD reliably indexing consciousness); content is the same but phrased more strongly/precisely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study proposes a new approach to integrate literature-derived information with genomic data to study microbial traits. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents a scalable approach that integrates literature-extracted information with genomic data to predict microbial phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the contribution as scalable and explicitly tied to phenotype prediction, but the core contribution (integrating literature and genomic data to study microbial traits) is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We fine-tuned specialized transformer-based large language models to analyze 3.3 million open-access scientific articles, extracting a network of phenotypic information linked to bacterial strains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We fine-tuned transformer-based language models to analyze 3.83 million open-access scientific articles, extracting a phenotypic network of bacterial strains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same modeling and extraction approach but with a different (larger) corpus size and slightly different wording, so the claim is clarified without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings align with known phenotypes and reveal novel correlations, leading to the identification of microbial genes relevant in particular disease and host-association phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings align with known phenotypes, reveal novel correlations, and uncover genes involved in disease and host associations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same result about alignment with known phenotypes and novel gene-phenotype correlations with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of machine learning methods to uncover cross-species patterns in microbial gene-phenotype correlations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates the potential of machine learning for uncovering cross-species gene-phenotype patterns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the concluding claim about the potential of ML to uncover cross-species patterns with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local GC content can capture significant association signals related to autism spectrum disorder (ASD) from noncoding variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Local GC content can capture significant association signals related to autism spectrum disorder (ASD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about local GC content capturing ASD association signals with near-identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The association signal for ASD appears driven by variants from male proband-female sibling pairs that are upstream of assigned genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The association signal for ASD appears driven by variants from male proband-female sibling pairs that are upstream of assigned genes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same directional result but reduces stated certainty from high to medium, so content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Expression Neighborhood Sequence Association Study (ENSAS) identifies gene expression-based neighborhoods that show significant ASD association signals, enriched for synapse-related gene ontology terms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Expression Neighborhood Sequence Association Study (ENSAS) identifies gene expression-based neighborhoods that show significant ASD association signals, enriched for synapse-related gene ontology terms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this ENSAS result verbatim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chromatin states annotations of variants are predictive of the proband-sibling local GC content differences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chromatin state annotations of variants are predictive of the proband-sibling local GC content differences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same finding and confidence about chromatin state annotations predicting GC content differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work provides new insights into associations of non-coding de novo mutations in ASD and presents an analytical framework applicable to other phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a new analytical framework for understanding the disease impact of de novo mutations, applicable to other phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's contribution more explicitly as an analytical framework for disease impact of de novo mutations; the core contribution is the same but more explicitly worded.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cryogenic electron tomography (cryoET) can determine in situ biological structures of molecular complexes at near atomic resolution by averaging half a million subtomograms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cryogenic electron tomography (cryoET) can determine in situ biological structures of molecular complexes at near-atomic resolution by averaging half a million subtomograms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the technical result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TomoNet is a software package developed to streamline the entire pipeline of cryoET and subtomogram averaging to achieve high resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TomoNet is a software package developed to carry out the entire pipeline of cryoET and subtomogram averaging to achieve high resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution claim about TomoNet with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TomoNet features built-in automatic particle picking and 3D classification functions that enhance the efficiency of high-resolution subtomogram averaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TomoNet features built-in automatic particle picking and three-dimensional classification functions to streamline high-resolution subtomogram averaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same functional features and intended effect on subtomogram averaging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applications of TomoNet demonstrate its capability for efficient and accurate particle picking on flexible and imperfect lattices. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applications of TomoNet demonstrate its capability of efficient and accurate particle picking on flexible and imperfect lattices. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The application-oriented result is preserved verbatim in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TomoNet has potential for broad applications to various cryoET projects targeting high-resolution in situ structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TomoNet has potential for broad applications to various cryoET projects targeting high-resolution in situ structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding statement about TomoNet's potential applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BRAF-mutant melanomas can enter a persister state that allows them to evade treatment and repopulate the tumor when reactivated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resistance of BRAF-mutant melanomas to targeted therapy arises from the ability of cells to enter a persister state and repopulate the tumor when reactivated. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core mechanistic result about persister states enabling resistance and repopulation with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The persister state in BRAF-mutant melanomas is characterized by increased oxidative phosphorylation, decreased proliferation, and increased invasive capacity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The persister state showed increased oxidative phosphorylation, decreased proliferation, and increased invasive capacity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same phenotypic characterization of the persister state with matching confidence and terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phylogenetic tracing identifies both intrinsic and acquired resistance mechanisms in BRAF-mutant melanomas. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phylogenetic tracing identified intrinsic and acquired resistance mechanisms and suggested specific temporal windows of potential therapeutic susceptibility. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the phylogenetic tracing finding but adds interpretation about temporal windows for therapeutic susceptibility, clarifying and slightly broadening the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The juxtaposition of transcriptomics and histology data allows for the identification of phenotypically-distinct populations in melanoma using imaging data alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-enabled analysis of histopathologic slides revealed morphologic features correlating with specific cell states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same capability but frames it specifically as a deep learning-enabled analysis of histopathology; the core result is preserved but the methodological emphasis is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The choice and timing of therapeutic agents significantly impact the ability to eradicate resistant clones in melanoma treatment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study defined state change and lineage selection during melanoma treatment with spatiotemporal resolution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's more action-oriented therapeutic claim into a descriptive conclusion about defining state changes and lineage selection; the clinical-action inference about choice/timing is thus downplayed and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The μBrain atlas facilitates knowledge translation between molecular and anatomical descriptions of the prenatal developing brain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The μBrain atlas provides a tool to comprehensively map early brain development across domains, model systems, and resolution scales. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the atlas contribution but frames it more broadly as a comprehensive mapping tool; the statement is more measured in certainty (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differences in the rates of expansion across cortical areas during gestation respect anatomical and evolutionary boundaries between cortical types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a spatial coupling between areal differences in the timing of neurogenesis and rates of neocortical expansion during gestation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims concern areal rates of expansion during gestation, but the publication emphasizes spatial coupling between timing and expansion rather than the specific statement about anatomical/evolutionary boundaries, so the focus is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of genes that are upregulated from mid-gestation and highly expressed in rapidly expanding neocortex are implicated in genetic disorders with cognitive sequelae. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genes that are upregulated from mid-gestation are highly expressed in rapidly expanding neocortex and are implicated in genetic disorders with cognitive sequelae. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the gene expression and disease-implication finding but reports it with higher stated certainty (medium -> high), so content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings demonstrate a spatial coupling between areal differences in the timing of neurogenesis and rates of expansion across the neocortical sheet during the prenatal epoch. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a spatial coupling between areal differences in the timing of neurogenesis and rates of neocortical expansion during gestation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint conclusion is carried over into the publication as the same spatial coupling result with comparable wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All skeletal proportions are highly heritable (∼40-50%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not report or restate a direct heritability estimate for all skeletal proportions, so this specific numerical heritability claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) identified 179 independent loci associated with skeletal proportions, of which 102 loci were not associated with height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies specific genetic variants that affect skeletal proportions (SPs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the GWAS-identification result but reports it more generically as identification of specific variants without the preprint's detailed locus counts, i.e., a narrowed/less detailed presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phenotypic and polygenic risk score analyses identified specific associations between osteoarthritis (OA) of the hip and knee and skeletal proportions of the corresponding regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polygenic score analysis reveals specific associations between osteoarthritis and hip and knee SPs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same association finding but reports it with higher certainty, amplifying the confidence from medium to high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loci associated with skeletal proportions are significantly enriched in human accelerated regions (HARs) and regulatory elements of genes differentially expressed through development between humans and great apes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SP loci are enriched in human accelerated regions and in regulatory elements of genes that are differentially expressed between humans and great apes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same enrichment result but presents it as a conclusion with reduced stated certainty (high -> medium), so the content is preserved but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study validates the use of deep learning models on DXA images to identify novel and specific genetic variants affecting the human skeletal form. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies specific genetic variants that affect skeletal proportions (SPs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the identification of genetic variants as a result but does not explicitly foreground the claim about validating deep learning on DXA; thus the methodological/conclusion emphasis is shifted (revised) relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Protein encodings do not substantially impact the binding predictions, with no statistically significant difference in binding affinity for KIBA in the investigated metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Protein encodings do not substantially impact the binding predictions, with no statistically significant difference in binding affinity for KIBA in the investigated metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim text and certainty are effectively identical, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant differences are seen for ligand encodings with random ligands and random ligand node properties, suggesting a much bigger reliance on ligand data for the learning tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant differences are seen for ligand encodings with random ligands and random ligand node properties, suggesting a much bigger reliance on ligand data for the learning tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim between versions with unchanged certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using different ways to combine protein and ligand encodings did not show a significant change in performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using different ways to combine protein and ligand encodings did not show a significant change in performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No meaningful textual or substantive differences were found between the preprint and publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SEMPAI outperforms state-of-the-art biomarkers in six of seven predictive tasks, including those with scarce data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SEMPAI outperforms state-of-the-art biomarkers in six of seven prediction tasks, including those with scarce data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SEMPAI’s DL models with integrated priors are superior to those without priors and to prior-only machine learning approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SEMPAI's deep learning models with integrated priors are superior to those without priors and to prior-only approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SEMPAI allows hypothesis testing and provides interpretable feedback about the origin of biological information in MPM images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LKANs outperformed both baseline and CKANs on almost all datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LKANs outperformed both baseline and CKANs on almost all datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CKANs can achieve comparable results but struggle with scaling over a large number of parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of KAN layers correlates with the model performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of KAN layers correlates with the model performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Linear KANs show promising results in improving the performance of deep learning models with relatively small number of parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linear KANs show promising results in improving the performance of deep learning models with a relatively small number of parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unleashing KAN potential in different SOTA deep learning architectures currently used in genomics requires further research. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unleashing KAN potential in different state-of-the-art deep learning architectures currently used in genomics requires further research. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bioinformatics Tool Recommendation system (BTR) is an innovative deep learning model designed to recommend the most suitable tools for a given workflow-in-progress. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bioinformatics Tool Recommendation system (BTR) is a deep learning model designed to recommend suitable tools for a given workflow-in-progress. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: BTR leverages graph neural network technology to represent the workflow as a graph, capturing essential context. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that BTR outperforms the existing Galaxy tool recommendation system. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The G0-Premature Chromosome Condensation (G0-PCC) technique combined with the Rapid Automated Biodosimetry Tool-II (RABiT-II) provides a faster alternative for assessing radiation exposure in large-scale emergencies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a lower concentration of Calyculin A (Cal A) than recommended effectively increased the yield of highly-condensed G0-PCC cells (hPCC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a concentration of phosphatase inhibitor lower than recommended significantly increases the yield of cells with highly condensed chromosomes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a correlation between radiation dose and the extent of hPCC chromosome fragmentation, enabling automation of radiation damage quantification using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The same subspaces of neural activity represent task-relevant information across multiple tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The same subspaces of neural activity represent task-relevant information across multiple tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural recordings found that task-relevant information about stimulus features and motor actions were represented in subspaces of neural activity that were shared across tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Task-relevant information about stimulus features and motor actions were represented in subspaces of neural activity that were shared across tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Monkeys' internal belief about the current task predicted the strength of representations in task-relevant subspaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Monkeys adapted to changes in the task by iteratively updating their internal belief about the current task. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain can flexibly perform multiple tasks by compositionally combining task-relevant neural representations across tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain can flexibly perform multiple tasks by compositionally combining task-relevant neural representations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core claim is retained but the publication lowers stated certainty and slightly shortens wording, clarifying rather than changing the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AL inhibitory network suppresses shared chemical compounds while enhancing responses to distinct compounds during odor learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AL inhibitory network suppresses responses to shared chemical compounds while enhancing responses to distinct compounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly adjusted but the result and scope are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This suppression and enhancement lead to improved pattern separation and a more concise neural code. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This results in improved pattern separation and a more concise neural code. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same mechanistic consequence with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Calcium imaging data support the predictions regarding the role of inhibitory plasticity in the early olfactory network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Calcium imaging data support the predictions regarding the role of inhibitory plasticity in the AL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same evidence claim with only a minor wording change (AL vs early olfactory network).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A graph convolutional neural network performing an odor categorization task revealed a similar mechanism for contrast enhancement. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A graph convolutional neural network performing an odor categorization task revealed a similar mechanism for contrast enhancement. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The computational result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides insights into how inhibitory plasticity reshapes the coding for efficient learning of complex odors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides insights into how inhibitory plasticity in the early olfactory network reshapes the coding for efficient learning of complex odors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the network (early olfactory) and presents the conclusion with stronger stated certainty, clarifying and amplifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stacking algorithm can predict held-out brain activity better or at least as well as the individual encoding models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stacking algorithm can predict held-out brain activity better or at least as well as the individual encoding models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is repeated without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The weights of the linear combination in the stacking algorithm are readily interpretable and show the importance of each feature space for predicting a voxel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The weights of the linear combination in the stacking algorithm are readily interpretable and show the importance of each feature space for predicting a voxel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological/result claim verbatim in meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Structured variance partitioning allows for targeted questions about the similarity between feature spaces and brain regions despite correlations between the feature spaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Structured variance partitioning allows for targeted questions about the similarity between feature spaces and brain regions even in the presence of correlations between the feature spaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording adjusted but the methodological conclusion remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods can be useful for researchers interested in aligning brain activity with different layers of a neural network or with other types of correlated feature spaces. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods can be useful for researchers interested in aligning brain activity with different layers of a neural network or with other types of correlated feature spaces. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The innovative strategy for simultaneous multi-omics analysis by direct infusion (SMAD) allows quantification of over 9,000 metabolite m/z features and over 1,300 proteins from the same sample in less than five minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMAD allows quantification of over 9000 metabolite m/z features and over 1300 proteins from the same sample in less than 5 min. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numbers, scope and claim are the same; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The requirement for different LC-MS platforms for each omics layer limits throughput and increases costs, hindering the application of mass spectrometry-based multi-omics to large scale drug discovery or clinical cohorts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This limitation/critique present in the preprint does not appear among the publication claims provided, so it appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficiency and reliability of the SMAD method were validated through practical applications, including mouse macrophage M1/M2 polarization and high throughput drug screening in human 293T cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficiency and reliability of SMAD were validated with three case studies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Relationships between proteomic and metabolomic data are discovered by machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Relationships between proteomic and metabolomic data are discovered by machine learning and validated. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit validation of the discovered relationships, strengthening the claim's support and thus increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The receptor protein displays a heterogeneous diffusion behavior that goes beyond the ensemble level, with individual trajectories exhibiting more than one diffusive state. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The receptor protein displays a heterogeneous diffusion behavior that goes beyond the ensemble level, with individual trajectories exhibiting more than one diffusive state. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is repeated verbatim in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-state Brownian-obstructed diffusion model can account for the experimentally observed anomalous diffusion of the population and the heterogeneous single-molecule behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-state Brownian-obstructed diffusion model can account for the experimentally observed anomalous diffusion of the population and the heterogeneous single-molecule behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concatenated multi-network system evaluates and selects those physical models that most accurately describe the receptor’s translational diffusion. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concatenated multi-network system evaluates and selects those physical models that most accurately describe the receptor's translational diffusion. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the experimentally observed trajectories (72.5% to 88.7% for α-bungarotoxin-labeled receptor and between 73.5% and 90.3% for antibody-labeled molecules) are accurately described by the two-state model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of the experimentally observed trajectories (72.5 to 88.7% for α-bungarotoxin-labeled receptor and between 73.5 and 90.3% for antibody-labeled molecules) are accurately described by the two-state model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only ∼15% of the trajectories fit to the fractional Brownian motion model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only ~15% of the trajectories fit to the fractional Brownian motion model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This quantitative result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The self-supervised learning method (SSL) for pixel classification provides a more streamlined cell segmentation approach that requires no dataset-specific modifications or curated labelled data sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The self-supervised learning method (SSL) for pixel classification does not require parameter tuning or curated data sets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our SSL algorithm meets the criteria of being fully automated and demonstrates versatility across various magnifications, optical modalities, and cell types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm meets the criteria of being fully automated with versatility across various magnifications, optical modalities, and cell types.
