alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SSL technique achieved consistent F1 scores ranging from 0.831 to 0.876, outperforming the Cellpose algorithm, which had scores ranging from 0.645 to 0.8815.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SSL technique displayed consistently high F1 scores across segmented cell images, ranging from 0.771 to 0.888.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different (wider) F1 ranges and removes the explicit superior-outperforming framing, so the numeric claim and comparative strength are revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On average, the SSL method achieved an F1 score of 0.852 ±0.017, exceeding Cellpose’s average of 0.804 ±0.08.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SSL algorithm matches or outperforms the popular Cellpose algorithm, which showed a greater F1 variance due to more false negatives.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific averaged superiority is softened in the publication to 'matches or outperforms' with discussion of variance, indicating a tempered comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel SSL method minimizes the need for extensive computational expertise and data security concerns, facilitating the incorporation of automated segmentation into biological research.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the claims about reduced computational expertise needs or data security implications found in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural network surrogates can reduce and accelerate quantitative systems pharmacology (QsP) models by training machine learning approximations on simulations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network surrogates reduce and accelerate QsP models by training ML approximations on simulations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution that neural surrogates accelerate QsP by learning from simulations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common neural network methodologies exhibit training instabilities similar to those of explicit solvers of ordinary differential equations (ODEs) when applied to stiff QsP models.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the claim comparing instabilities of common NN methods to explicit ODE solvers for stiff models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The continuous-time echo state network (CTESN) can accurately train and predict on stiff QsP models where other methods fail.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The continuous-time echo state network (CTESN) serves as the implicit analogue of ML architectures for surrogatizing production QsP models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the CTESN as an implicit analogue for surrogates rather than explicitly claiming it succeeds where others fail, clarifying the role and softening the superiority claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CTESN can accelerate QsP simulations by up to 56 times while achieving less than 5% relative error in all tested examples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CTESN can accelerate QsP simulations by up to 5.2x compared to optimized Differential Equations.jl solvers while maintaining less than 5% relative error.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the <5% error claim but substantially revises the reported acceleration factor (56x -> 5.2x), narrowing the magnitude of the claimed speedup.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating the numerical properties of QsP methods into machine learning can improve the intersection between the two fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating the numerical properties of QsP methods into machine learning can improve the intersection of these fields.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding recommendation about integrating numerical properties into ML for QsP.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We pioneered a deep graph neural network based method for predicting protein stability change upon mutation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed gated graph neural network-based approach predicts changes in protein stability upon mutation.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core contribution: a graph neural network approach to predict stability changes on mutation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed method showed competitive high performance in data generalization and bias suppression with ultra-low time consumption.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach addresses issues of anti-symmetric bias and generalization to unseen examples in previous machine learning techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on addressing anti-symmetric bias and generalization, which clarifies the aspects of bias suppression and generalization claimed in the preprint (less emphasis on the 'ultra-low time' phrasing).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was applied to predict the Pyrazinamide’s Gibbs free energy change for a real case study.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the stated real-case application to Pyrazinamide Gibbs free energy change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general bird detection model achieves over 65% recall and 50% precision on novel aerial data without any local training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general bird detection model achieves over 65% recall and 50% precision on novel aerial data without any local training.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuning the general model with only 1000 local annotations increases recall to an average of 84% and precision to 69%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning the general model with only 1000 local annotations increases recall to an average of 84% and precision to 69%.
alignments.rationale: Identical fine-tuning performance claim is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retraining from the general model improves local predictions even when moderately large annotation sets are available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Retraining from the general model improves local predictions even when moderately large annotation sets are available.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this conclusion about benefits of retraining from the general model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: General models for detecting broad classes of organisms using airborne imagery are achievable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: General models for detecting broad classes of organisms using airborne imagery are achievable.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this high-level conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models can reduce the effort, expertise, and computational resources necessary for automating the detection of individual organisms across large scales.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These models can reduce the effort, expertise, and computational resources necessary for automating the detection of individual organisms across large scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claimed practical benefits for reducing effort and resources.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The graph neural network model PLANET demonstrates comparable scoring power to other deep learning models that rely on 3D protein-ligand complex structures as input.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph neural network model PLANET demonstrates comparable scoring power to the best results from other deep learning models when tested on the CASF-2016 benchmark.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the benchmark (CASF-2016), clarifying the context in which comparable scoring power was observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PLANET shows notably better performance in virtual screening trials on the DUD-E and LIT-PCBA benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PLANET's performance in virtual screening trials on the DUD-E benchmark is notably better than several deep learning and machine learning models.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms superior performance on DUD-E but omits explicit mention of LIT-PCBA, narrowing the original benchmark claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PLANET achieved comparable accuracy on LIT-PCBA as the conventional docking program Glide while requiring less than 1% of the computation time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PLANET achieves comparable accuracy to the conventional docking program Glide while using less than 1% of Glide's computation time.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the specific performance-computation tradeoff claim relative to Glide.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PLANET exhibited decent performance in binding affinity prediction, making it potentially useful for drug discovery in practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the general 'decent performance in binding affinity prediction' conclusion; instead it adds details about multi-objective training, so the practical claim is not retained as stated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chemogenetic manipulations in male mice demonstrated inhibitory effects of the CRH+ BLA-NAc projection on reward behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CRH+/GABA BLA-NAc projection influences reward behaviors in males but not in females.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the male-specific effect on reward but omits the explicit phrasing that the effect is 'inhibitory', altering the mechanistic wording rather than the subject/scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neither excitation nor inhibition of the CRH+ BLA-NAc projection influenced female reward behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CRH+/GABA BLA-NAc projection influences reward behaviors in males but not in females.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert lack of influence on female reward behaviors; the publication summarizes this as a male-specific effect, consistent with the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive whole-brain mapping uncovered significant differences in NAc innervation patterns that were both sex and ELA-dependent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive whole-brain mapping uncovered significant differences in NAc innervation patterns that were both sex and ELA dependent.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim, but the publication notes a reduced certainty level (medium), so confidence is tempered relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CRH/GABA BLA-NAc projection that influences reward behaviors in males differs structurally and functionally in females.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CRH+/GABA BLA-NAc projection that influences reward behaviors in males differs structurally and functionally in females.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this structural/functional sex-difference conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 models multiple Na<sub>V</sub> channel conformations, including those from experimental structures, new states not yet experimentally identified, and potential intermediate states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 models multiple NaV channel conformations, including those observed in experimental structures, states that have not been described experimentally, and potential intermediate states.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this modeling capability with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold Multimer models Na<sub>V</sub> complexes with auxiliary β-subunits and calmodulin with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold Multimer models NaV complexes with auxiliary β-subunits and calmodulin with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this claim forward unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of protein partners significantly alters the conformational landscape of the Na<sub>V</sub> α-subunit.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of protein partners significantly alters both the modeled conformational landscape of the NaV α-subunit and the coupling between its functional states.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the preprint claim by adding effects on coupling between functional states, clarifying and broadening the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of deep learning-based methods to expand our understanding of Na<sub>V</sub> channel structure, gating, and modulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight the potential of deep learning-based methods to expand our understanding of NaV channel structure, gating, and modulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-limb Optogenetic Motor Mapping (MOMM) consistently revealed cortical topographies for all mapped features within and across mice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-limb Optogenetic Motor Mapping (MOMM) consistently revealed cortical topographies for all mapped features within and across mice.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many motor maps overlapped and were topographically similar.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many motor maps overlapped and were topographically similar.
alignments.rationale: This result is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cortex appears to be topographically organized by motor programs, which are responsible for coordinated, multi-limbed, and behavioral-like movements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cortex appears to be topographically organized by motor programs, which are responsible for coordinated, multi-limbed, and behavior-like movements.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about program-based topography is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several motor movement representations extended beyond cytoarchitecturally defined somatomotor cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several motor movement representations extended beyond cytoarchitecturally defined somatomotor cortex.
alignments.rationale: The observation about map extent beyond somatomotor cortex is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The disentangled variational autoencoder (DSVAE) captures significant differences in temporal step distance between schizophrenia patients and control subjects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Context embeddings from the DSVAE more accurately separate windows from schizophrenia patients and control subjects compared to the standard time-resolved functional connectivity (tr-FC) approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The context embeddings produced by the DSVAE model more accurately separate windows from schizophrenia patients and control subjects than baseline models and the standard time-resolved functional connectivity (tr-FC) approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the baseline for comparison by explicitly including other baseline models in addition to tr-FC, broadening the comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For individuals with schizophrenia, the context embedding space of the DSVAE is significantly correlated with both age and symptom severity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For individuals with schizophrenia, the context embedding space of the DSVAE model is significantly correlated with both age and symptom severity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with schizophrenia spend more time in three distinct clusters of functional network connectivity, indicating altered connectivity patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with schizophrenia spend more time in three distinct clusters of functional network connectivity, indicating varying levels of connectivity with control subjects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DSVAE captures features that are complementary to standard tr-FC features, broadening the neuroimaging toolset for analyzing fMRI dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DSVAE model captures features that are complementary to standard tr-FC features, broadening the neuroimaging toolset for analyzing fMRI dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about complementarity with tr-FC is preserved verbatim and with similar certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: In vitro assays show that SARS-CoV-2 3CLpro cleaves NEMO with fine-tuned efficiency.

Matching confidence is slightly lower due to formatting/encoding differences in the publication text provided.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning- and physics-based computational methods predict that variation in key binding residues of 3CLpro-NEMO helps explain the high fitness of SARS-CoV-2 in humans.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Cleavage of NEMO is an important piece of information to be accounted for in the pathology of COVID-19.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The top 10 journals are responsible for 28.64% of all published documents. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Strong collaborative relationships are present, along with small, isolated collaboration networks of individual institutions. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI research activities in the fields of sports pedagogy, sports sociology, and sports economics seem to have played a subordinate role thus far. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI research activities in the fields of sports pedagogy, sports sociology, and sports economics seem to have played a subordinate role thus far. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding observation about subordinate roles of these subfields is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PARP1-specific support vector machine-based regressor, when employing PLEC fingerprints, achieved a high Normalized Enrichment Factor at the top 1% on the hardest test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PARP1-specific support vector machine-based regressor using PLEC fingerprints achieved a high Normalized Enrichment Factor at the top 1% on the hardest test set. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present the first PARP1-specific machine-learning scoring functions for structure-based virtual screening. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The preprint's novelty/contribution claim about being the 'first' is not asserted in the publication; instead the paper draws a more general conclusion about ML scoring functions' effectiveness, reducing the emphasis on novelty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of PARP1-specific machine-learning models performed better than generic and classical scoring functions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified scoring function was more predictive than any other investigated scoring function, including the classical scoring function used as a baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader claim (majority outperforming) is narrowed in the publication to a single identified scoring function being most predictive, and the publication states this with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Employing protein-ligand-extracted fingerprints as molecular descriptors led to the best-performing and most-efficient model for predicting PARP1 inhibitors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PARP1-specific support vector machine-based regressor using PLEC fingerprints achieved a high Normalized Enrichment Factor at the top 1% on the hardest test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the PLEC-SVM result; this clarifies and supports the preprint's statement that protein-ligand-extracted fingerprints produced the best-performing model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning performed poorly on this target in comparison with the simpler ML models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or discuss the comparative poor performance of deep learning models present in the preprint; that claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multiscale transformer model demonstrates foundational capabilities including the prediction of essential genes, genetic variant effects, regulatory element activity, and taxonomy of unannotated sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The megaDNA model demonstrates foundational capabilities including the prediction of essential genes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model generates de novo sequences up to 96K base pairs that contain functional regulatory elements and novel proteins with phage-related functions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model was pre-trained on unannotated bacteriophage genomes with byte-level tokenization. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A linear mixed effects model can be applied to analyze and quantify genetic Boolean logic gate performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A linear mixed effects model can be applied to analyze and quantify genetic Boolean logic gate performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fixed effect, β, serves as a general descriptor of the Boolean nature of a circuit. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Point estimates of the fixed effect, β̂, are appropriate descriptors of gate behavior that can be used to statistically evaluate performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the claim to specify point estimates β̂ and their use in statistical evaluation, clarifying the preprint's statement about β as a descriptor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a linear correlation between β and the predicted translation rate, indicating its utility for the forward design of new Boolean gates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is an association between β̂ and the predicted translation rate. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the preprint's claim from a linear correlation with strong-design implications to a more modest 'association' with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study recommends sample sizes for evaluating gate performance based on Monte Carlo simulations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the preprint's specific sample-size recommendations from Monte Carlo simulations; that practical recommendation is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The degeneration of midbrain dopamine neurons disrupts the neural control of natural behavior in Parkinson's disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The degeneration of midbrain dopamine neurons disrupts the neural control of natural behavior in Parkinson's disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fast behavioral modules with higher velocities are more vulnerable to loss of dopamine and are preferentially affected at early stages of Parkinsonism. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Levodopa effectively improves the velocity of behavioral modules in parkinsonian animals, but does not affect their usage and transition probability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful responses to PD-1 inhibition are characterized by an induction of B cell receptor (BCR) clonal diversity after treatment initiation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: aPD-1-induced BCR diversity is validated as a predictor of clinical response in a larger cohort of glioblastoma, melanoma, and head and neck squamous cell carcinoma patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Active pre-treatment immune infiltration is important for chemotherapy success. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Active pre-treatment immune infiltration is important for chemotherapy success. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the importance of pre-treatment immune infiltration is consistently presented in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DECODEM and DECODEMi are publicly available to facilitate studying the role of the TME in mediating response in various cancer indications and treatments. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DECODEM and DECODEMi are publicly available to facilitate studying the role of the TME in mediating response in various cancer treatments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the availability claim but narrows wording to 'treatments' (omitting 'indications') and increases certainty about availability and utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In young adults, better sleep quality, mild depressive symptoms, and GMV of several brain regions were associated with better motor performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Better sleep quality, mild depressive symptoms, and altered grey matter volume in specific brain regions are associated with better motor performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the association but slightly rephrases GMV as 'altered grey matter volume in specific brain regions', clarifying anatomical specificity while keeping the association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HCP-YA analyses showed progressively stronger associations between motor performance and each domain: depressive symptoms, sleep quality, and GMV. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The association between sleep quality, depressive symptoms, and grey matter volume with motor performance was conceptually replicated in a young adult sample. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim about progressively stronger associations in HCP-YA is reported in the publication more conservatively as a conceptual replication in a young adult sample, removing the emphasis on progressive strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining sleep and depressive symptoms significantly improved the canonical correlations related to motor performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific preprint claim about combined predictors improving canonical correlations is not reported in the publication and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In HCP-Aging, better sleep quality, fewer depressive symptoms, and increased GMV were associated with motor performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased grey matter volume is associated with better motor performance across all samples studied. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a GMV–motor performance association across all samples rather than the preprint's specific multi-factor HCP-Aging result; this shifts focus to GMV and generalizes across cohorts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Robust multivariate associations were observed between sleep quality, depressive symptoms, and GMV with motor performance, as well as age-related variations in these factors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Age-related variations exist in the multivariate associations between sleep quality, depressive symptoms, grey matter volume, and motor performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report age-related variation in the multivariate relationships; the publication frames this explicitly as age-related variations in those multivariate associations, which clarifies the preprint's statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study found moderate to good correlation across the three data sets for identifying reproductive toxicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The yeast (S. cerevisiae) and nematode (C. elegans) high-throughput assays demonstrate moderate to good correlation in identifying toxicants. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The yeast and nematode high-throughput assays demonstrated significant concordance with mammalian reproductive toxicity data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation coefficients between the high-throughput BMDs and the ToxRefDB BMDs indicate strong agreement in reproductive toxicity assessment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the concordance using specific metrics (BMDs vs ToxRefDB), clarifying the evidence without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings underscore the potential of high-throughput assays to identify environmental chemicals that exhibit reproductive toxicity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore the potential of high-throughput assays to identify environmental chemicals that exhibit reproductive toxicity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried forward verbatim from preprint to publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating high-throughput datasets into mammalian in vivo prediction models could enhance their predictive value in rapid screening efforts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: TUBB3+ nerve density decreased with aging, with the frontal bone demonstrating earlier onset age-related nerve loss than the parietal bone. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Nerves in the periosteum and dura mater exhibited similar yet distinct temporal patterns of nerve growth and loss. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An EEG waveform derivative - a modified theta-to-delta ratio (EEG Ratio) - accurately tracks real-time Ca<sup>2+</sup><sub>MCH</sub> in rats. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Cocaine self-administration and long-term withdrawal altered the EEGTDT ratio, suggesting shortening and circadian redistribution of synchronous MCH neuron activities. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Features of the EEGTDT ratio indicative of prolonged synchronous MCH neuron activities predicted lower subsequent cocaine seeking. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The preprint phrased this as a prediction/hypothesis while the publication states it more assertively as an ability; the claim's confidence/stance is thus amplified and slightly revised in tone.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatiotemporal measurements of neuronal calcium, metabolism, and brain blood-oxygen can be accurately modeled from a low-dimensional state-space reconstructed from the time history of pupil diameter.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The seconds-scale spatiotemporal dynamics of neuronal calcium, metabolism, and brain blood oxygen can be accurately modeled from a low-dimensional, nonlinear manifold reconstructed from pupil diameter.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies seconds-scale dynamics and refers to a nonlinear manifold rather than a generic state-space, clarifying and slightly sharpening the modeling claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to integrate diverse experimental data into a unified generative model via mappings from an intrinsic arousal manifold has been demonstrated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reframing arousal as a latent dynamical system provides a new perspective on fluctuations in brain, body, and behavior across modalities, contexts, and scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from demonstrating a specific modeling capability to the broader conceptual reframing of arousal as a latent dynamical system, which clarifies and broadens the stated contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spontaneous, spatially structured fluctuations in brain-wide physiology are largely reflections of an arousal-related process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spontaneous, spatially structured fluctuations in brain-wide physiology on timescales of seconds are largely expressions of a low-dimensional, organism-wide dynamical system.
alignments.rationale: The publication tightens the characterization by describing these fluctuations as expressions of a specific low-dimensional dynamical system, clarifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based approaches did not perform better than a simple additive model for combinatorial perturbations of two genes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: None of the models outperformed the baselines, which highlights the importance of critical benchmarking in directing and evaluating method development.
alignments.rationale: The publication's conclusion that models did not beat baselines matches the preprint's negative result about deep-learning approaches relative to a simple additive model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For perturbations of genes that had not yet been seen, the deep learning-based approaches did not outperform the baseline of predicting the mean across the training perturbations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Five foundation models and two other deep learning models were compared against deliberately simple baselines for predicting transcriptome changes after single or double perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly describes the experimental comparison that supports the preprint result about failure to generalize to unseen perturbations; this clarifies the context and methods of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple linear model reliably outperforms all other models when pre-trained on another perturbation dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: None of the models outperformed the baselines, which highlights the importance of critical benchmarking in directing and evaluating method development.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the empirical observation that simple baselines outperform more complex models and uses it to motivate benchmarking, clarifying the implication of the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The poor performance of deep learning models is partially due to the pre-training data being observational.
alignments.rationale: The hypothesis about observational pre-training data as a partial cause is not present in the publication claims, suggesting it was not carried forward; mapping confidence is moderate because the idea could be discussed but not asserted as a claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work highlights the need for clear setting of objectives and for critical benchmarking to direct research efforts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: None of the models outperformed the baselines, which highlights the importance of critical benchmarking in directing and evaluating method development.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly draws the same conclusion about the importance of benchmarking, matching the preprint contribution claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent research in deep-learning-based foundation models promises to learn representations of single-cell data that enable prediction of the effects of genetic perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broader contextual claim about the promise of foundation models for single-cell data that was not present as an explicit preprint claim, so it is added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies consistent architectural heterogeneity of extracellular vesicles (EVs) using cryogenic transmission electron microscopy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies consistent architectural heterogeneity of extracellular vesicles (EVs) using cryogenic transmission electron microscopy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is identical between preprint and publication; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A structural atlas of dominantly consistent shapes of EVs was identified across different sources such as cancer cells, normal cells, and body fluids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A structural atlas of dominantly consistent shapes of EVs was identified across different sources, including cancer cells and body fluids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim content and scope are the same though phrasing is slightly tightened; meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average eccentricity of the imaged EVs was found to be 0.5366, and the average equivalent diameter was 132.43 nm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average eccentricity of the imaged EVs was found to be 0.5366 ± 0.2, and the average equivalent diameter was 132.43 ± 67 nm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds uncertainty (±) to the reported metrics, clarifying measurement variability without changing the claim's scope or directional conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architectural heterogeneity of EVs was consistent across all sources and independent of purification techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architectural heterogeneity of EVs was consistent across all sources and independent of purification techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same across versions; only minor metadata label difference (result vs conclusion) but content and strength are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study will serve as a reference foundation for high-resolution EV images and offer insights into their potential biological impact. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study will serve as a reference foundation for high-resolution images of EVs and offer insights into their potential biological impact. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Minor rewording only; the contribution and confidence level are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FOXA1 binding is strongly affected by co-binding TFs AP-1 and CEBPB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FOXA1 binding is strongly affected by co-binding transcription factors AP-1 and CEBPB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording expanded (TFs -> transcription factors) but content, scope, and confidence are identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FOXA1 and AP-1 show binding cooperativity in vitro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FOXA1 and AP-1 show binding cooperativity in vitro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FOXA1's binding is determined more by local sequences than chromatin context, including eu-/heterochromatin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FOXA1's binding is determined more by local sequences than chromatin context, including eu-/heterochromatin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change in wording or meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AP-1 is partially responsible for differential binding of FOXA1 in different cell types. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AP-1 is partially responsible for differential binding of FOXA1 in different cell types. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a framework for elucidating genetic rules underlying PF binding specificity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a framework for elucidating genetic rules underlying PF binding specificity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A shallow convolutional neural network based on the Drosophila visual system predicts defensive blinking to looming objects in infants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A shallow convolutional neural network based on the Drosophila visual system predicts defensive blinking to looming objects in infants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional neural network predicts superior colliculus responses to optical expansion in adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network predicts superior colliculus responses to optical expansion in adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Minor wording change (convolutional -> neural) but the scientific content and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The responses of the convolutional network to a broad array of naturalistic video clips predict self-reported emotion largely on the basis of subjective arousal. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network's responses to naturalistic video clips predict self-reported emotion largely by way of subjective arousal. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same predictive finding reworded slightly; scope and confidence preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings illustrate how motor and experiential components of human emotion relate to species-general systems for survival in unpredictable environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings illustrate how a simple neural network architecture optimized for a species-general task relevant for survival explains motor and experiential components of human emotion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conclusion more strongly (uses 'explains' vs 'relate') and raises certainty; this is a substantive strengthening and clearer explanatory claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Delta GCaMP6 power in the deprived visual cortex decreased, suggesting that excitatory activity was reduced in the region. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Following monocular deprivation, Δ band (0.4-4 Hz) GCaMP6 activity in the deprived visual cortex decreased. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the frequency band (clarification) and drops the interpretive phrase linking the decrease directly to reduced excitatory activity; removing that inference tempers interpretive confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interhemispheric visual homotopic functional connectivity was rapidly reduced by the disruption of visual drive through monocular deprivation and was sustained significantly below baseline state. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interhemispheric visual homotopic functional connectivity decreased following monocular deprivation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication shortens wording but conveys the same result; phrasing is more concise without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reduction of visual homotopic connectivity was accompanied by a reduction in parietal and motor homotopic connectivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was a reduction in parietal and motor homotopic connectivity after monocular deprivation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result expressed slightly differently; meaning and confidence preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhanced internetwork connectivity between visual and parietal cortex peaked at MD2. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhanced internetwork connectivity between the visual and parietal cortex peaked 2 days after monocular deprivation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: MD2 is restated as '2 days after' with no substantive change in claim content or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings support the hypothesis that early monocular deprivation induces dynamic reorganization of disparate functional networks including association cortices. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings support the hypothesis that early monocular deprivation induces dynamic reorganization of disparate functional networks including the association cortices. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is preserved verbatim aside from minor article insertion; content and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial and temporal parameters of arm movements are independently encoded in the low-dimensional trajectories of population activity in motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial and temporal parameters of arm movements are independently encoded in the low-dimensional trajectories of population activity in motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each movement's direction corresponds to a fixed neural trajectory through neural state space and its speed to how quickly that trajectory is traversed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each movement's direction corresponds to a fixed neural trajectory through neural state space and its speed to how quickly that trajectory is traversed. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recurrent neural network models demonstrate that this coding allows independent control over the spatial and temporal parameters of movement by separate network parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recurrent neural network models demonstrate that this coding allows independent control over the spatial and temporal parameters of movement by separate network parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results support a key prediction of the dynamical systems view of motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results support a key prediction of the dynamical systems view of motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Not all parameters of movement are defined by different trajectories of population activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Not all parameters of movement are defined by different trajectories of population activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim content and confidence are preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AnnoSpat demonstrates superior performance in rapid and accurate annotation of cell types compared to alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AnnoSpat demonstrates higher performance in rapid and accurate annotation of cell types compared to alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of AnnoSpat to various cohorts recapitulated known islet pathobiology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of AnnoSpat to various cohorts recapitulates known islet pathobiology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim (recapitulation of known islet pathobiology) with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential dynamics of pancreatic polypeptide (PP) cell abundance and CD8+ T cells infiltration in islets were observed during type 1 diabetes progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential dynamics of pancreatic polypeptide (PP) cell abundance and CD8+ T cells infiltration in islets are observed during type 1 diabetes progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observed differential dynamics with the same specificity and certainty; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCGN outperformed existing methods by 5% and achieved 10 times higher prediction accuracy than the counterpart model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCGN consistently exhibited the best performance with a median PCC of 0.232. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the specific relative improvements (5%, 10x) with a concrete performance metric (median PCC=0.232); this revises the quantitative claim and presents a less hyperbolic, narrower performance statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCGN is interpretable in recognizing special cell morphology and cell-cell interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCGN can be extended to handle bulk RNA-seq data while providing interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains a claim of interpretability but frames it in a different context (extension to bulk RNA-seq) and omits the specific claim about recognizing morphology and cell-cell interactions, thus narrowing and tempering the original interpretability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCGN does not require complex building graph preprocessing compared to existing methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about avoiding complex graph preprocessing appears in the publication; related efficiency claims (e.g., parameter count) are new but do not explicitly restate this preprocessing claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provides a theoretical basis for future modeling of multi-modal data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCGN can be extended to handle bulk RNA-seq data while providing interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more concrete, implementational extension (bulk RNA-seq) rather than the preprint's broader theoretical-basis claim, clarifying and narrowing the original forward-looking statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCGN can accurately predict important biomarkers for breast cancer from cheaper histopathological images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCGN is a powerful tool for inferring gene expressions from histopathological images in precision health applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific breast-cancer biomarker prediction claim as a broader capability to infer gene expression from histopathology for precision health, clarifying the application and broadening scope beyond the original specific claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A reinforcement learning approach performs favourably in comparison with a one-step ahead optimisation algorithm and a model predictive controller for the inference of bacterial growth parameters in a simulated chemostat. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A reinforcement learning approach performs favourably in comparison with a one-step ahead optimisation algorithm and a model predictive controller for the inference of bacterial growth parameters in a simulated chemostat. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance result verbatim, with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning demonstrates the ability to train over a distribution of parameters, indicating that this approach is robust to parametric uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement learning demonstrates the ability to train over a distribution of parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the training-over-distribution result but separates the explicit robustness interpretation into a distinct claim, effectively clarifying the observational result from its interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach allows uncertainty in model parameterisation to be directly incorporated into the search for optimal experiments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach indicates robustness to parametric uncertainty. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the methodological conclusion from an explicit capability to incorporate parameter uncertainty into experimental search to a more general statement about indicating robustness, which revises and slightly narrows the original procedural claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method presented is complementary to existing optimisation approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No explicit statement in the publication asserts complementarity to existing optimisation approaches; that interpretive claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence has a fundamental role to play in the future of optimal experimental design. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this broad, normative claim about the fundamental role of AI; it focuses on specific results and interpretations instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAGES is a method that describes expression data using features calculated from sequence-based prediction methods and 3D structural models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAGES is a method that describes expression data using features calculated from sequence-based prediction methods and 3D structural models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact contribution claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified prominent expression of intrinsically disordered regions in breast cancer proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified prominent expression of intrinsically disordered regions in breast cancer proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are relationships between drug perturbation signatures and breast cancer disease signatures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are relationships between drug perturbation signatures and breast cancer disease signatures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same observed relationships with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAGES is generally applicable to describe diverse biological phenomena including disease states and drug effects. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAGES is generally applicable to describe diverse biological phenomena including disease states and drug effects. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same generalizability claim with equivalent caveats and low certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study shows the emergence of functional subtypes of senescence in primary dermal fibroblasts. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding as enabled by a specific analysis framework (SenSCOUT) rather than only stating the emergence of subtypes; this clarifies the result by emphasizing the enabling method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Eleven distinct morphology clusters of senescent cells were defined, with their abundance dependent on the mode of senescence induction, time post-induction, and age of the donor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Single-cell morphology analysis revealed 11 distinct morphology clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three bona-fide senescence subtypes (C7, C10, C11) were identified, with C10 showing the strongest age-dependence across a cohort of fifty aging individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework provided can be applied for next-generation senotherapy screens and may explain heterogeneous senescence phenotypes across biological settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes and elevates the contribution by naming and characterizing SenSCOUT as a robust framework for identifying/classifying subtypes, which clarifies the preprint's proposed framework and increases the stated confidence in its utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibitory tone increases during high cognitive load and situations of uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibitory tone increases during situations of difficult discrimination tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integrated Ising-type model can explain how the brain may utilize inhibition to improve its decision-making accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integrated Ising-type model can explain how the brain may utilize inhibition to improve its decision-making accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The regime of the brain's decision-making activity is in proximity to a critical transition line between the ordered and disordered phases. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MICAH integrates metabolic and phylogenetic relationships among microbes into a heterogeneous graph representation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MICAH's generalizability and reproducibility are demonstrated through its application to intratumoral bacterial data across 5 cancer types and 5 fungi datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning framework demonstrates the highest area under the curve (AUC) of 0.93 in classifying ER+/ER- patients compared to six widely used machine learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning method shows advantages for metabolomics based breast cancer ER status classification, with both the highest prediction accuracy and better revelation of disease biology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Scupa effectively identifies polarized cells across different species and experimental conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
